data.description: AllTalk is based on the Coqui TTS engine, similar to the Coqui_tts extension for Text generation webUI, however supports a variety of advanced features, such as a settings page, low VRAM support, DeepSpeed, narrator, model finetuning, custom models, wav file maintenance. It can also be used with 3rd Party software via JSON calls.  - GitHub - erew123/alltalk_tts: AllTalk is based on the Coqui TTS engine, similar to the Coqui_tts extension for Text generation webUI, however supports a variety of advanced features, such as a settings page, low VRAM support, DeepSpeed, narrator, model finetuning, custom models, wav file maintenance. It can also be used with 3rd Party software via JSON calls.

time. As a solo enthusiast (not a business or team) my resources are inherently limited. This project has been one of my passions, but I must balance it with other commitments.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo manage AllTalk sustainably, I prioritize support requests based on their overall impact and the number of users affected. I encourage you to utilize the comprehensive documentation and engage with the AllTalk community discussion area. These resources often provide immediate answers and foster a supportive user network.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eShould your inquiry extend beyond the documentation, especially if it concerns a bug or feature request, I assure you I’ll offer my best support as my schedule permits. However, please be prepared for varying response times, reflective of the personal dedication I bring to AllTalk. Your understanding and patience in this regard are greatly appreciated.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIt's important to note that \u003cstrong\u003eI am not\u003c/strong\u003e the developer of any TTS models utilized by AllTalk, nor do I claim to be an expert on them, including understanding all their nuances, issues, and quirks. For specific TTS model concerns, I’ve provided links to the original developers in the Help section for direct assistance.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThank you for your continued support and understanding.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e💖 Showing Your Support\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--showing-your-support\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 💖 Showing Your Support\" href=\"#-showing-your-support\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf AllTalk has been helpful to you, consider showing your support through a donation on my \u003ca href=\"https://ko-fi.com/erew123\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eKo-fi page\u003c/a\u003e. on Windows 10/11 and Linux. Instructional videos for both setup processes are linked below.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEnsure that \u003cstrong\u003eGit\u003c/strong\u003e is installed on your system as it is required for cloning the repository. If you do not have Git installed, visit \u003ca href=\"https://git-scm.com/downloads\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGit's official website\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e to download and install it.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows users must install C++ development tools for Python to compile Python packages. Detailed information and a link to these tools can be found in the help section \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts#-help-with-problems\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWindows \u0026amp; Python requirements for compiling packages\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eQUICK SETUP - Text-Generation-webui\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor a step-by-step video guide, click \u003ca href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=icn2XS5rUH8\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo set up AllTalk within Text-generation-webui, follow either method:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDownload AllTalk Setup\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVia Terminal/Console (Recommended)\u003c/strong\u003e:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd \\text-generation-webui\\extensions\\\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003egit clone https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVia Releases Page (Cannot be automatically updated after install as its not linked to Github)\u003c/strong\u003e:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDownload the latest \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts.zip\u003c/code\u003e from \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/releases\"\u003eReleases\u003c/a\u003e and extract it to \u003ccode\u003e\\text-generation-webui\\extensions\\alltalk_tts\\\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStart Python Environment\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn the text-generation-webui folder, start the environment with the appropriate command:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003ecmd_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're unfamiliar with Python environments and wish to learn more, consider reviewing \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Python Environments Simplified\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRun AllTalk Setup Script\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to the AllTalk directory and execute the setup script:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd extensions\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003eatsetup.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./atsetup.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInstall Requirements\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFollow the on-screen instructions to install the necessary requirements. It's recommended to test AllTalk's functionality before installing DeepSpeed.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNote\u003c/strong\u003e: Always activate the Text-generation-webui Python environment before making any adjustments or using Fine-tuning. Additional instructions for Fine-tuning and DeepSpeed can be found within the setup utility and on this documentation page.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eQUICK SETUP - Standalone Installation\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo perform a Standalone installation of AllTalk:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGet AllTalk Setup\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVia Terminal/Console (Recommended)\u003c/strong\u003e:\n\u003cul \u003ccode\u003egit clone https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVia Releases Page (Cannot be href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/releases\"\u003eReleases\u003c/a\u003e and extract it to your chosen directory, for example, run the setup script:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003eatsetup.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./atsetup.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFollow the Setup Prompts\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSelect Standalone Installation and then Option 1 and follow any on-screen instructions to install the required files. DeepSpeed is automatically installed on \u003cstrong\u003eWindows\u003c/strong\u003e based system, but will only work on Nvidia GPU's. \u003cstrong\u003eLinux\u003c/strong\u003e based system users will have to follow the DeepSpeed installation instructions.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're unfamiliar with Python environments and wish to learn more, consider reviewing \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Python Environments Simplified\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eImportant\u003c/strong\u003e: Do not use spaces in your folder path (e.g. avoid \u003ccode\u003e/my folder-is-this/alltalk_tts-main\u003c/code\u003e) as this causes issues with Python \u0026amp; Conda.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRefer to \u003ccode\u003e🟩 Other installation notes\u003c/code\u003e for further details, including information on additional voices, changing IP, character card notes etc.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you wish to understand AllTalks start-up screen, please read \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding the AllTalk start-up screen\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟩 Docker Builds and Google Colab's\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--docker-builds-and-google-colabs\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟩 Docker Builds and Google Colab's\" href=\"#-docker-builds-and-google-colabs\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile an AllTalk Docker build exists, it's important to note that this version is based on an earlier iteration of AllTalk and was set up by a third party. At some point, my goal is to deepen my understanding of Docker and its compatibility with AllTalk. This exploration may lead to significant updates to AllTalk to ensure a seamless Docker experience. However, as of now, the Docker build should be considered a BETA version and isn't directly supported by me.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAs for Google Colab, there is partial compatibility with AllTalk, though with some quirks. I am currently investigating these issues and figuring out the necessary adjustments to enhance the integration. Please read \u003cstrong\u003eCustom Python environments Limitations Notice\u003c/strong\u003e below this section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo run AllTalk as a standalone application with a custom Python environment, ensure you install AllTalk's requirements into the environment of your choice. The instructions provided are generalized due to the variety of potential Python environments.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePython Compatibility\u003c/strong\u003e: The TTS engine requires Python \u003cstrong\u003e3.9.x\u003c/strong\u003e to \u003cstrong\u003e3.11.x\u003c/strong\u003e. AllTalk is tested with Python \u003cstrong\u003e3.11.x\u003c/strong\u003e. \u003ca href=\"https://pypi.org/project/TTS/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eSee TTS Engine details\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePath Names\u003c/strong\u003e: Avoid spaces in path names as this can cause issues.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCustom Python Environments\u003c/strong\u003e: If encountering issues potentially related to a custom environment, consider testing AllTalk with the quick setup standalone method that builds its own environment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" Setup\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEnsure \u003ccode\u003epython --version\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003epip\u003c/code\u003e commands work in your terminal. Install Python and Pip if necessary.\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://www.python.org/downloads/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ePython Installation\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/installation/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ePip Installation\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInstall Miniconda\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDownload and install Miniconda for your OS from the \u003ca href=\"https://docs.conda.io/projects/miniconda/en/latest/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eMiniconda Website\u003c/a\u003e. Use the \u003ccode\u003eAnaconda Prompt\u003c/code\u003e from the Start Menu or Application Launcher to access the base Conda environment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eClone AllTalk Repository\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to your desired folder (e.g., \u003ccode\u003ec:\\myfiles\\\u003c/code\u003e) and clone the AllTalk repository:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003egit clone https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMove to the AllTalk folder with the following commands:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCreate Conda Environment\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eCreate a Conda environment named \u003ccode\u003ealltalkenv\u003c/code\u003e with Python 3.11.5:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003econda create --name alltalkenv python=3.11.5\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eActivate the new environment:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003econda activate alltalkenv\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInstall Requirements\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInstall dependencies based on your machine type:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFor Windows: \u003ccode\u003epip install -r system\\requirements\\requirements_standalone.txt\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFor Linux/Mac: \u003ccode\u003epip install -r system/requirements/requirements_standalone.txt\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStart AllTalk\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eRun AllTalk with the following:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003epython script.py\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNote\u003c/strong\u003e: For updates, DeepSpeed installations, or other modifications, always activate the \u003ccode\u003ealltalkenv\u003c/code\u003e Conda environment first. Custom scripts or batch files can simplify launching AllTalk.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟩 \u003cstrong\u003eCustom Python environments Limitations Notice\u003c/strong\u003e: Given the vast array of Python environments and custom configurations out there, it's challenging for me to guarantee comprehensive support for each unique setup. AllTalk leverages a wide range of scripts and libraries, many of which are developed and maintained outside of my control. As a result, these components might not always behave as expected in every custom Python environment. I'll do my best to assist where I can, but please understand that my ability to help with issues stemming from these external factors may be limited.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟩 Other installation notes\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--other-installation-notes\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟩 Other installation notes\" href=\"#-other-installation-notes\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOn first startup, AllTalk will download the Coqui XTTSv2 2.0.2 model to its \u003cstrong\u003emodels\u003c/strong\u003e folder (1.8GB space required). Check the command prompt/terminal window if you want to know what its doing. After it says \"Model Loaded\" the Text generation webUI is usually available on its IP address a few seconds later, for you to connect to in your browser. If you are running a headless system and need to change the IP, please see the Help with problems section down below.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOnce the extension is loaded, please find all documentation and settings on the link provided in the interface (as shown in the screenshot below).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhere to find voices\u003c/strong\u003e \u003ca href=\"https://aiartes.com/voiceai\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://aiartes.com/voiceai\u003c/a\u003e or \u003ca href=\"https://commons.wikimedia.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://commons.wikimedia.org/\u003c/a\u003e or interviews on youtube etc. Instructions on how to cut down and prepare a voice sample are within the built in documentation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePlease read the note below about start-up times and also the note about ensuring your character cards are set up \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts#-a-note-on-character-cards--greeting-messages\"\u003ecorrectly\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSome extra voices for AllTalk are downloadable \u003ca href=\"https://drive.google.com/file/d/1bYdZdr3L69kmzUN3vSiqZmLRD7-A3M47/view?usp=drive_link\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CPnx1rpkuKvVj5fGr9OiUJHZ_e8DfTzP/view\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟩 Changing AllTalks IP address \u0026amp; Accessing AllTalk over your Network\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--changing-alltalks-ip-address--accessing-alltalk-over-your-network\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟩 Changing AllTalks IP address \u0026amp; Accessing AllTalk over your Network\" href=\"#-changing-alltalks-ip-address--accessing-alltalk-over-your-network\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eClick to expand\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAllTalk is coded to start on 127.0.0.1, meaning that it will ONLY be accessable to the local computer it is running on. If you want to make AllTalk available to other systems on your network, you will need to change its IP address to match the IP address of your network card/computers current IP address. There are 2x ways to change the IP address:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eStart AllTalk and within its web interface and you can edit the IP address on the \"AllTalk Startup Settings\".\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eYou can edit the \u003ccode\u003econfignew.json\u003c/code\u003efile in a text editor and change \u003ccode\u003e\"ip_address\": \"127.0.0.1\",\u003c/code\u003e to the IP address of your choosing.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSo, for example, if your computer's network card was on IP address 192.168.0.20, you would change AllTalk's setting to 192.168.1.20 and then \u003cstrong\u003erestart\u003c/strong\u003e AllTalk. You will need to ensure your machine stays on this IP address each time it is restarted, by setting your machine to have a static IP address.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟩 Text-geneneration-webui \u0026amp; Stable-Diffusion Plugin - Load Order \u0026amp; stripped text\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--text-geneneration-webui--stable-diffusion-plugin---load-order--stripped-text\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟩 Text-geneneration-webui \u0026amp; Stable-Diffusion Plugin - Load Order \u0026amp; stripped text\" href=\"#-text-geneneration-webui--stable-diffusion-plugin---load-order--stripped-text\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eClick to expand\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Stable Diffusion plugin for Text-generation-webui \u003cstrong\u003estrips out\u003c/strong\u003e some of the text, which is passed to Stable Diffusion for image/scene generation. Because this text is stripped, its important to consider the load order of the plugins to get the desired result you want. Lets assume the AI has just generated the following message \u003ccode\u003e*He walks into the room with a smile on his face and says* Hello how are you?\u003c/code\u003e. Depending on the load order will change what text reaches AllTalk for generation e.g.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSD Plugin loaded before AllTalk\u003c/strong\u003e - Only \u003ccode\u003eHi how are you?\u003c/code\u003e is sent to AllTalk, with the \u003ccode\u003e*He walks into the room with a smile on his face and says*\u003c/code\u003e being sent over to SD for image generation. Narration of the scene is not possible.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cstrong\u003eAllTalk loaded before SD Plugin\u003c/strong\u003e - \u003ccode\u003e*He walks into the room with a smile on his face and says* Hello how are you?\u003c/code\u003e is sent to AllTalk with the \u003ccode\u003e*He walks into the room with a smile on his face and says*\u003c/code\u003e being sent over to SD for image generation.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nThe load order can be changed within Text-generation-webui's \u003ccode\u003esettings.yaml\u003c/code\u003e file or \u003ccode\u003ecmd_flags.txt\u003c/code\u003e (depending on how you are managing your extensions).\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/blob/main/atandsdplugin.jpg\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/atandsdplugin.jpg\" alt=\"image\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟩 A note on Character Cards \u0026amp; Greeting Messages\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--a-note-on-character-cards--greeting-messages\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟩 A note on Character Cards \u0026amp; Greeting Messages\" href=\"#-a-note-on-character-cards--greeting-messages\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eClick to expand\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMessages intended for the Narrator should be enclosed in asterisks \u003ccode\u003e*\u003c/code\u003e and those for the character inside quotation marks \u003ccode\u003e\"\u003c/code\u003e. However, AI systems often deviate from these rules, resulting in text that is neither in quotes nor asterisks. Sometimes, text may appear with only a single asterisk, and AI models may vary their formatting mid-conversation. For example, they might use asterisks initially and then switch to unmarked text. A properly formatted line should look like this:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e\"\u003c/code\u003eHey! I'm so excited to finally meet you. I've heard so many great things about you and I'm eager to pick your brain about computers.\u003ccode\u003e\"\u003c/code\u003e \u003ccode\u003e*\u003c/code\u003eShe walked across the room and picked up her cup of coffee\u003ccode\u003e*\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMost narrator/character systems switch voices upon encountering an asterisk or quotation marks, which is somewhat effective. AllTalk has undergone several revisions in its sentence splitting and identification methods. While some irregularities and AI deviations in message formatting are inevitable, any line beginning or ending with an asterisk should now be recognized as Narrator dialogue. Lines enclosed in double quotes are identified as Character dialogue. For any other text, you can choose how AllTalk handles it: whether it should be interpreted as Character or Narrator dialogue (most AI systems tend to lean more towards one format when generating text not enclosed in quotes or asterisks).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWith improvements to the splitter/processor, I'm confident it's functioning well. You can monitor what AllTalk identifies as Narrator lines on the command line and adjust its behavior if needed (Text Not Inside - Function).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟩 I want to know more about the XTTS AI model used\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--i-want-to-know-more-about-the-xtts-ai-model-used\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟩 I want to know more about the XTTS AI model used\" href=\"#-i-want-to-know-more-about-the-xtts-ai-model-used\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eClick to expand\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCurrently the XTTS model is the main model used by AllTalk for TTS generation. If you want to know more details about the XTTS model, its capabilties or its technical features you can look at resources such as:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://docs.coqui.ai/en/latest/models/xtts.html\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://docs.coqui.ai/en/latest/models/xtts.html\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS\"\u003ehttps://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS/discussions\"\u003ehttps://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS/discussions\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟪 Updating\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--updating\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟪 Updating\" href=\"#-updating\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMaintaining the latest version of your setup ensures access to new features and improvements. Below are the steps to update your installation, whether you're using Text-Generation-webui or running as a Standalone Application.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNOTE\u003c/strong\u003e Future updates will be handled by using the \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e utility.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cstrong\u003eNOTE\u003c/strong\u003e If you have an install \u003cstrong\u003eprior to 28th March 2024\u003c/strong\u003e that you are updating, perform the \u003ccode\u003egit pull\u003c/code\u003e instructions below, then run the \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e utility and select option 1 in either the Standalone ot Text-generation-webui menu (as matches your system).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eUPDATING - Text-Generation-webui\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe update process closely mirrors the installation steps. Follow these to ensure your setup remains current:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOpen a Command Prompt/Terminal\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to your Text-Generation-webui folder with:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd text-generation-webui\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStart the Python Environment\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eActivate the Python environment tailored for your operating system. Use the appropriate command from below based on your OS:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003ecmd_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emacOS: \u003ccode\u003ecmd_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux): \u003ccode\u003ecmd_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're unfamiliar with Python environments and wish to learn more, consider reviewing \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Python Environments Simplified\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNavigate to the AllTalk TTS Folder\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMove into your extensions and then the alltalk_tts directory:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd extensions/alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUpdate the Repository\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFetch the latest updates from the repository with:\n\u003cul and then migrating your data:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRename Existing Directory\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFirst, rename your current \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e folder to keep it safe e.g. \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts.old\u003c/code\u003e. This preserves any existing data.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFollow the Quick Setup instructions\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou will now follow the \u003cstrong\u003eQuick Setup\u003c/strong\u003e instructions, performing the \u003ccode\u003egit clone https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e to pull down a new copy of AllTalk and install the requirements.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're not familiar with Python environments, see \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Python Environments Simplified\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section for more info.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eMigrate Your Data\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBefore\u003c/strong\u003e starting the AllTalk, transfer the \u003ccode\u003emodels\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003evoices\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eoutputs\u003c/code\u003e folders and also \u003ccode\u003econfignew.json\u003c/code\u003e from \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts.old\u003c/code\u003e to the new \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e directory. This action preserves your voice history and prevents the need to re-download the model.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003col start=\"4\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLaunch AllTalk\u003c/strong\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eYou're now ready to launch AllTalk and check it works correctly.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003col start=\"6\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFinal Step\u003c/strong\u003e:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOnce you've verified that everything is working as expected and you're satisfied with the setup, feel free to delete the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts.old\u003c/code\u003e directory to free up space.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNvidia CUDA Development Toolkit Installation\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe toolkit is crucial for DeepSpeed to compile/build for your version of Linux and requires around 3GB's of disk space.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInstall using your package manager \u003cstrong\u003e(Recommended)\u003c/strong\u003e e.g. \u003cstrong\u003eCUDA Toolkit 11.8\u003c/strong\u003e or download directly from \u003ca href=\"https://developer.nvidia.com/cuda-toolkit-archive\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eNvidia CUDA Toolkit Archive\u003c/a\u003e (choose 11.8 or 12.1 for Linux).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOpen a Terminal Console\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAfter Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit installation, access your terminal console.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInstall libaio-dev\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eUse your Linux distribution's package manager.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003esudo apt install libaio-dev\u003c/code\u003e for Debian-based systems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003esudo yum install libaio-devel\u003c/code\u003e for RPM-based systems.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNavigate to Text generation webUI Folder\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eChange directory to your Text generation webUI folder with \u003ccode\u003ecd text-generation-webui\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eActivate Text generation webUI Custom Conda Environment\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eRun \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e to start the environment.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're unfamiliar with Python environments and wish to learn more, consider reviewing \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Python Environments Simplified\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSet \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e Environment Variable\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDeepSpeed locates the Nvidia toolkit using the \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e environment variable.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eYou will only set this temporarily as Text generation webUI sets up its own CUDA_HOME environment each time you use \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e./start_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTemporarily Configuring \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen the Text generation webUI Python environment is active \u003cstrong\u003e(step 5)\u003c/strong\u003e, set \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport PATH=${CUDA_HOME}/bin:${PATH}\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${CUDA_HOME}/lib64:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can confirm the path is set correctly and working by running the command \u003ccode\u003envcc --version\u003c/code\u003e should confirm \u003ccode\u003eCuda compilation tools, release 11.8.\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIncorrect path settings may lead to errors. If you encounter path issues or receive errors like \u003ccode\u003e[Errno 2] No such file or directory\u003c/code\u003e when you run the next step, confirm the path correctness or adjust as necessary.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDeepSpeed Installation\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInstall DeepSpeed using \u003ccode\u003epip install deepspeed\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTroubleshooting\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTroubleshooting steps for DeepSpeed installation can be located down below.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNOTE\u003c/strong\u003e: You \u003cstrong\u003eDO NOT\u003c/strong\u003e need to set Text-generation-webUI's \u003cstrong\u003e--deepspeed\u003c/strong\u003e setting for AllTalk to be able to use DeepSpeed. These are two completely separate things and incorrectly setting that on Text-generation-webUI may cause other complications.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eLinux DeepSpeed - Standalone Folder\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eChange directory to your AllTalk TTS folder with \u003ccode\u003ecd alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eActivate AllTalk Custom Conda Environment\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eRun \u003ccode\u003e./start_environment.sh\u003c/code\u003e to start the AllTalk Python \u003ccode\u003e./atsetup.sh\u003c/code\u003e.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're unfamiliar with Python Variable\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe DeepSpeed installation routine locates the Nvidia toolkit using the \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e environment variable. This can be set temporarily for a session or permanently, depending on other requirements you may have for other Python/System environments.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFor temporary use, proceed to \u003cstrong\u003estep 8\u003c/strong\u003e. For a permanent solution, see \u003ca href=\"https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-environments.html#set-env-vars\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eConda's manual on setting environment variables\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e(Optional) Permanent \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e Setup\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf you choose to set \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e permanently, follow the instructions in the provided Conda manual link above.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eConfiguring \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen your Python environment is active \u003cstrong\u003e(step 5)\u003c/strong\u003e, set \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport PATH=${CUDA_HOME}/bin:${PATH}\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${CUDA_HOME}/lib64:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can confirm the path is set correctly and working by running the command \u003ccode\u003envcc --version\u003c/code\u003e should confirm \u003ccode\u003eCuda compilation tools, release 11.8.\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIncorrect path settings may lead to errors. If you encounter path issues or receive errors like \u003ccode\u003e[Errno 2] No such file or directory\u003c/code\u003e when you run the next step, confirm the path correctness or adjust as necessary.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDeepSpeed Installation\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInstall DeepSpeed using \u003ccode\u003epip install deepspeed\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStarting AllTalk TTS WebUI\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLaunch the AllTalk TTS interface with \u003ccode\u003e./start_alltalk.sh\u003c/code\u003e and enable DeepSpeed.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf setting \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e results in path duplication errors (e.g., \u003ccode\u003e.../bin/bin/nvcc\u003c/code\u003e), you can correct this by unsetting \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e with \u003ccode\u003eunset CUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e and then adding the correct path to your system's PATH variable.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAlways verify paths and compatibility with other CUDA-dependent applications to avoid conflicts.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf you have multiple versions of the Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit installed, you will have to specify the version number in step 8 for the CUDA_HOME path.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf it became necessary to uninstall DeepSpeed, you can do so by start the Python enviroment and then running \u003ccode\u003epip uninstall deepspeed\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" Pre-compiled wheel files for specific Python, CUDA and Pytorch builds, or manually compiling DeepSpeed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eWindows DeepSpeed - Pre-Compiled Wheels (Quick and Easy)\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eDeepSpeed Installation with Pre-compiled Wheels\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-deepspeed-installation-with-pre-compiled-wheels\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: DeepSpeed Installation with Pre-compiled Wheels\" href=\"#deepspeed-installation-with-pre-compiled-wheels\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIntroduction to Pre-compiled Wheels\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe \u003ccode\u003eatsetup.bat\u003c/code\u003e utility simplifies the installation of DeepSpeed by automatically downloading and installing pre-compiled wheel files. These files are tailored for \u003cstrong\u003especific\u003c/strong\u003e versions of Python, CUDA, and PyTorch, ensuring compatibility with both the \u003cstrong\u003eStandalone Installation\u003c/strong\u003e and a standard build of \u003cstrong\u003eText-generation-webui\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eManual Installation of Pre-compiled Wheels\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf needed, pre-compiled DeepSpeed wheel files that I have built are available on the \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/releases\"\u003eReleases Page\u003c/a\u003e. You can manually install or uninstall these wheels using the following commands:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInstallation: \u003ccode\u003epip install {deep-speed-wheel-file-name-here}\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUninstallation: \u003ccode\u003epip uninstall deepspeed\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUsing \u003ccode\u003eatsetup.bat\u003c/code\u003e for Simplified Management\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFor those running the Standalone Installation or a standard build of Text-generation-webui, the \u003ccode\u003eatsetup.bat\u003c/code\u003e utility offers the simplest and most efficient way to manage DeepSpeed installations on Windows.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eWindows DeepSpeed - Manual Compilation\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eManual compilation of DeepSpeed wheels is an advanced process that requires:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e1-2 hours\u003c/strong\u003e of your time for initial setup and compilation.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e6-10GB\u003c/strong\u003e of disk space on your computer.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eA solid technical understanding of Windows environments and Python.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Wheel Compatibility\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eA compiled DeepSpeed wheel is uniquely tied to the specific versions of Python, PyTorch, and CUDA used during its compilation. If any of these versions are changed, you will need to compile a new DeepSpeed wheel to ensure compatibility.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCompiling DeepSpee Resources\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMyself and \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/S95Sedan\"\u003e@S95Sedan\u003c/a\u003e have worked to simplify the compilation process. \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/S95Sedan\"\u003e@S95Sedan\u003c/a\u003e has notably improved the process for later versions of DeepSpeed, ensuring ease of build on Windows.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBecause \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/S95Sedan\"\u003e@S95Sedan\u003c/a\u003e is now maintaining the instructions for compiling DeepSpeed on Windows, please visit \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/S95Sedan\"\u003e@S95Sedan\u003c/a\u003e's\u003cbr\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/S95Sedan/Deepspeed-Windows\"\u003eDeepSpeed GitHub page\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🆘 Support Requests, Troubleshooting \u0026amp; Many common questions and troubleshooting steps are covered here.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSearch Past Discussions:\u003c/strong\u003e Your issue or question might already have been addressed in the discussions area or \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed\"\u003eclosed issues\u003c/a\u003e. Please use the search function to see if there's an existing solution or advice that applies to your situation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBug Reports:\u003c/strong\u003e If you've encountered what you believe is a bug, please first check the \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/25\" data-hovercard-type=\"issue\" data-hovercard-url=\"/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/25/hovercard\"\u003eUpdates \u0026amp; Bug Fixes List\u003c/a\u003e to see if it's a known issue or one that's already been resolved. If not, I encourage you to report it by raising a bug report in the \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues\"\u003eIssues section\u003c/a\u003e, providing as much detail as possible to help identify and fix the issue.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFeature Requests:\u003c/strong\u003e The current Feature request list can be \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/discussions/74\" data-hovercard-type=\"discussion\" data-hovercard-url=\"/erew123/alltalk_tts/discussions/74/hovercard\"\u003efound here\u003c/a\u003e. file\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you are on a Windows machine or a Linux machine, you should be able to use the \u003ccode\u003eatsetup.bat\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e./atsetup.sh\u003c/code\u003e utility to create a diagnositcs file. If you are unable to use the \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e utility, please follow the instructions below.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eManually making a diagnostics report file\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOpen a command prompt window and start the Python environment. Depending on your setup (Text-generation-webui or Standalone AllTalk), the steps to start the Python environment vary:\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFor Text-generation-webui Users\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to the Text-generation-webui directory:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd text-generation-webui\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eStart the Python environment suitable for your OS:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003ecmd_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emacOS: \u003ccode\u003ecmd_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux): \u003ccode\u003ecmd_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMove into the AllTalk directory:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd extensions/alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFor Standalone AllTalk Users\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e folder:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eStart the Python environment:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003estart_environment.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./start_environment.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're unfamiliar with Python environments and wish to learn more, consider reviewing \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Python Environments Simplified\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003col start=\"2\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRun the diagnostics and select the requirements file name you installed AllTalk with:\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003epython diagnostics.py\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou will have an on screen output showing your environment setttings, file versions request vs whats installed and details of your graphics card (if Nvidia). This will also create a file called \u003ccode\u003ediagnostics.log\u003c/code\u003e in the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e folder, that you can upload if you need to create a support ticket on here.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" Simplified\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThink of Python environments like different rooms in your house, each designed for a specific purpose. Just as you wouldn't cook in the bathroom or sleep in the kitchen, different Python applications need their own \"spaces\" or environments because they have unique requirements. Sometimes, these requirements can clash with those of other applications (imagine trying to cook a meal in a bathroom!). To avoid this, you can create separate Python environments.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhy Separate Environments?\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-why-separate-environments\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Why Separate Environments?\" href=\"#why-separate-environments\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSeparate environments, like separate rooms, keep everything organized and prevent conflicts. For instance, one Python application might need a specific version of a library or dependency, while another requires a different version. Just as you wouldn't store kitchen utensils in the bathroom, you wouldn't want these conflicting requirements to interfere with each other. Each environment is tailored and customized for its application, ensuring it has everything it needs without disrupting others.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow It Works in Practice:\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-it-works-in-practice\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How It Works in Practice:\" href=\"#how-it-works-in-practice\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStandalone AllTalk Installation:\u003c/strong\u003e When you install AllTalk standalone, it's akin to adding a new room to your house specifically designed for your AllTalk activities. The setup process, using the atsetup utility, constructs this custom \"room\" (Python environment \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_environment\u003c/code\u003e) with all the necessary tools and furnishings (libraries and dependencies) that AllTalk needs to function smoothly, without meddling with the rest of your \"house\" (computer system). The AllTalk environment is started each time you run \u003ccode\u003estart_alltalk\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003estart_environment\u003c/code\u003e within the AllTalk folder.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eText-generation-webui Installation:\u003c/strong\u003e Similarly, installing Text-generation-webui is like setting up another specialized room. Upon installation, it automatically creates its own tailored environment, equipped with everything required for text generation, ensuring a seamless and conflict-free operation. The Text-generation-webui environment is started each time you run \u003ccode\u003estart_*your-os-version*\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003ecmd_*your-os-version*\u003c/code\u003e within the Text-generation-webui folder.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eManaging Environments:\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-managing-environments\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Managing Environments:\" href=\"#managing-environments\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eJust as you might renovate a room or bring in new furniture, you can also update or modify Python environments as needed. Tools like Conda or venv make it easy to manage these environments, allowing you to create, duplicate, activate, or delete them much like how you might manage different rooms in your house for comfort and functionality.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOnce you're in the right environment, by activating it, installing or updating dependencies (the tools and furniture of your Python application) is straightforward. Using pip, a package installer for Python, you can easily add what you need. For example, to install all required dependencies listed in a requirements.txt file, you'd use:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003epip install -r requirements.txt\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis command tells pip to read the list of required packages and versions from the requirements.txt file and install them in the current environment, ensuring your application has everything it needs to operate. It's like having a shopping list for outfitting a room and ensuring you have all the right items delivered and set up.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRemember, just as it's important to use the right tools for tasks in different rooms of your house, it's crucial to manage your Python environments and dependencies properly to ensure your applications run as intended.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow do I know if I am in a Python environment?:\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-do-i-know-if-i-am-in-a-python-environment\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How do I know if I am in a Python environment?:\" href=\"#how-do-i-know-if-i-am-in-a-python-environment\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" environment that it currently running within that terminal/console.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/blob/main/pythonenvironment.jpg\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/pythonenvironment.jpg\" alt=\"image\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 Windows \u0026amp; Python requirements for compiling packages \u003cstrong\u003e(ERROR: Could not build wheels for TTS)\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eERROR: Microsoft Visual C++ 14.0 or greater is required\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003eERROR: Could not build wheels for TTS.\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003eModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'TTS\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePython requires that you install C++ development tools on Windows. This is detailed on the \u003ca href=\"https://wiki.python.org/moin/WindowsCompilers\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ePython site here\u003c/a\u003e. You would need to install \u003ccode\u003eMSVCv142 - VS 2019 C++ x64/x86 build tools\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eWindows 10/11 SDK\u003c/code\u003e from the C++ Build tools section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can get hold of the \u003cstrong\u003eCommunity\u003c/strong\u003e edition \u003ca href=\"https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/downloads/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e the during installation, selecting \u003ccode\u003eC++ Build tools\u003c/code\u003e and then \u003ccode\u003eMSVCv142 - VS 2019 C++ x64/x86 build tools\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eWindows 10/11 SDK\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/pythonrequirementswindows.jpg\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/pythonrequirementswindows.jpg\" alt=\"image\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 Standalone Install - start_{youros}.xx opens and closes instantly and AllTalk doesnt start\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis is more than likely caused by having a space \u003ccode\u003e \u003c/code\u003e in your folder path e.g. \u003ccode\u003ec:\\program files\\alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e. In this circumstance you would be best moving the folder to a path without a space e.g. \u003ccode\u003ec:\\myfiles\\alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e. You would have to delete the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_environment\u003c/code\u003e folder and \u003ccode\u003estart_alltalk.bat\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003estart_alltalk.sh\u003c/code\u003e and then re-run \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e to re-create the environment and startup files.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 I think AllTalks requirements file has installed something another extension doesn't like\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIve paid very close attention to \u003cstrong\u003enot\u003c/strong\u003e impact what Text-generation-webui is requesting on a factory install. This is one of the requirements of submitting an extension to Text-generation-webui. If you want to look at a comparison of a factory fresh text-generation-webui installed packages (with cuda 12.1, though AllTalk's requirements were set on cuda 11.8) you can find that comparison \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/23\" data-hovercard-type=\"issue\" data-hovercard-url=\"/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/23/hovercard\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e. This comparison shows that AllTalk is requesting the same package version numbers as Text-generation-webui or even lower version numbers (meaning AllTalk will not update them to a later version). What other extensions do, I cant really account for that.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI will note that the TTS engine downgrades Pandas data validator to 1.5.3 though its unlikely to cause any issues. You can upgrade it back to text-generation-webui default (december 2023) with \u003ccode\u003epip install pandas==2.1.4\u003c/code\u003e when inside of the python environment. I have noticed no ill effects from it being a lower or higher version, as far as AllTalk goes. This is also the same behaviour as the Coqui_tts extension that comes with Text-generation-webui.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOther people are reporting issues with extensions not starting with errors about Pydantic e.g. \u003ccode\u003epydantic.errors.PydanticImportError: BaseSettings` has been moved to the pydantic-settings package. See https://docs.pydantic.dev/2.5/migration/#basesettings-has-moved-to-pydantic-settings for more details.\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIm not sure if the Pydantic version has been recently updated by the Text-generation-webui installer, but this is nothing to do with AllTalk. The other extension you are having an issue with, need to be updated to work with Pydantic 2.5.x. AllTalk was updated in mid december to work with 2.5.x. I am not specifically condoning doing this, as it may have other knock on effects, but within the text-gen Python environment, you can use \u003ccode\u003epip install pydantic==2.5.0\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003epip install pydantic==1.10.13\u003c/code\u003e to change the version of Pydantic installed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 I am having problems getting AllTalk to start after changing settings or making a custom setup/model setup.\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI would suggest following \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts#-problems-updating\"\u003eProblems Updating\u003c/a\u003e and if you still have issues after that, you can raise an issue \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\"
