Network\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou will need to change the IP address within AllTalk's settings from being 127.0.0.1, which only allows access from the local machine its installed on. To do this, please see \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#-changing-alltalks-ip-address--accessing-alltalk-over-your-network\"\u003eChanging AllTalks IP address \u0026amp; Accessing AllTalk over your Network\u003c/a\u003e at the top of this page.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou may also need to allow access through your firewall or Antivirus package to AllTalk.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 I am running a Headless system and need to change the IP Address manually as I cannot reach the config page\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo do this you can edit the \u003ccode\u003econfignew.json\u003c/code\u003e file within the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e folder. You would look for \u003ccode\u003e\"ip_address\": \"127.0.0.1\",\u003c/code\u003e and change the \u003ccode\u003e127.0.0.1\u003c/code\u003e to your chosen IP address,then save the file and start AllTalk.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen doing this, be careful not to impact the formatting of the JSON file. Worst case, you can re-download a fresh copy of \u003ccode\u003econfignew.json\u003c/code\u003e from this website and that will put you back to a factory setting.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eConfiguration and Usage Issues\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-configuration-and-usage-issues\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Configuration and Usage Issues\" href=\"#configuration-and-usage-issues\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 I activated DeepSpeed in the settings page, but I didnt install DeepSpeed yet and now I have issues starting up\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can either follow the \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts#-problems-updating\"\u003eProblems Updating\u003c/a\u003e and fresh install your config. Or you can edit the \u003ccode\u003econfignew.json\u003c/code\u003e file within the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e folder. You would look for '\"deepspeed_activate\": true,' and change the word true to false `\"deepspeed_activate\": false,' ,then save the file and try starting again.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you want to use DeepSpeed, you need an Nvidia Graphics card and to install DeepSpeed on your system. Instructions are \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts#-deepspeed-installation-options\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 I am having problems updating/some other issue where it wont start up/Im sure this is a bug\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePlease see \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts#-problems-updating\"\u003eProblems Updating\u003c/a\u003e. If that doesnt help you can raise an ticket \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e. It would be handy to have any log files from the console where your error is being shown. I can only losely support custom built Python environments and give general pointers. Please create a \u003ccode\u003ediagnostics.log\u003c/code\u003e report file to submit with a support request.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAlso, is your text-generation-webui up to date? \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui?tab=readme-ov-file#how-to-install\"\u003einstructions here\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 I see some red \"asyncio\" messages\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAs far as I am aware, these are to do with the chrome browser the gradio text-generation-webui in some way. I raised an issue about this on the text-generation-webui \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/issues/4788\" data-hovercard-type=\"issue\" data-hovercard-url=\"/oobabooga/textgen/issues/4788/hovercard\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e where you can see that AllTalk is not loaded and the messages persist. Either way, this is more a warning than an actual issue, so shouldnt affect any functionality of either AllTalk or text-generation-webui, they are more just an annoyance.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eStartup, Performance and Compatibility Issues\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-startup-performance-and-compatibility-issues\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Startup, Performance and Compatibility Issues\" href=\"#startup-performance-and-compatibility-issues\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 Understanding the AllTalk start-up screen\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe AllTalk start-up screen provides various bits of information about the detected Python environment and errors.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/alltalkstartup.jpg\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/alltalkstartup.jpg\" alt=\"image\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eConfig file check\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSometimes I need to add/remove something to your existing configuration file settings. Obviously, I don’t want to impact your existing settings, however any new features may need these settings to be created before AllTalk starts up. Ive added extra code that checks \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts/system/config/at_configupdate.json\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts/system/config/at_configdowngrade.json\u003c/code\u003e, either adding or removing items to your configuration as necessary. If a change is made, you will be notified and a backup of the previous configuration file will be created in the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e folder.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAllTalk startup Mode\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003einformational. This will state if AllTalk has detected it is running as part of Text-generation-webui or as a Standalone Application.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWAV file deletion\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf you have set deletion of old generated WAV files, this will state the time frame after which they are purged.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDeepSpeed version\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWhat version of DeepSpeed is installed/detected. This will \u003cstrong\u003enot\u003c/strong\u003e tell you if the version of DeepSpeed is compiled for your Python, PyTorch or CUDA version. Its important to remember that DeepSpeed has to be compiled for the exact version of Python, PyTorch and CUDA that you are using, so please ensure you have the correct DeepSpeed version installed if necessary.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eModel is available\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAllTalk is checking if your model files exist. This is not a validity check of the actual model files, they can still be corrupted. If files are missing, AllTalk will attempt to download them from Huggingface, however, if Huggingface has an outage/issue or your internet connection has issues, its possible corrupted or incomplete files will be downloaded. Please read \u003ccode\u003eRuntimeError: PytorchStreamReader failed reading zip archive: failed finding central directory\u003c/code\u003e if you need to confirm your model files are ok.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCurrent Python Version\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInformational. Literally tells you the version of Python running in your Python environment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCurrent PyTorch Version\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInformational. Tell tells you the version of \u003cstrong\u003ePyTorch\u003c/strong\u003e running in your Python environment, however if you have an Nvidia card, you should be running a CUDA based version of Pytorch. This is indicated with a \u003ccode\u003e+cXXX\u003c/code\u003e after the PyTorch version e.g. \u003ccode\u003e2.2.2+cu121\u003c/code\u003e would be PyTorch version 2.2.2 with CUDA 12.1 extensions. If you don’t have the PyTorch CUDA extensions installed, but you do have an Nvidia card, you may need to re-install PyTorch.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCurrent CUDA Version\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInformational. This is linked to the Current PyTorch Version, as detailed above.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCurrent TTS Version\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInformational. The current version of the TTS engine that is running.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAllTalk Github updated\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAs long as you have an internet connection, this will tell you last time AllTalk was updated on Github. It is checking the \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/commits/main/\"\u003ecommit list\u003c/a\u003e to see when the last commit was made. As such, this could be simply a documentation update, a bug fix or new features. Its simply there as a guide to let you know the last time something was changed on AllTalk's Github.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTTS Subprocess\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWhen AllTalk reaches this stage, the subprocess that loads in the AI model is starting. This is most likely where an error could occur with loading the TTS model, just after the documentation message.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAllTalk Settings \u0026amp; Documentation: http ://x.x.x.x\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe link where you can reach AllTalks built in settings and documentation page. The TTS model will be loading immediately after this is displayed.\n\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 AllTalk is only loading into CPU, but I have an Nvidia GPU so it should be loading into CUDA\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis is caused by Pytorch (Torch) not having the CUDA extensions installed (You can check by running the diagnostics). Typically this happens (on Standalone installations) because when the setup routine goes to install Pytorch with CUDA, it looks in the PIP cache and if a previous application has downloaded a version of Pytorch that \u003cstrong\u003edoesn't\u003c/strong\u003e have CUDA extensions, the PIP installer doesnt recognise this fact and just uses the cached version for installation. To resolve this:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOn the \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e utility, on the \u003ccode\u003eStandalone menu\u003c/code\u003e select to \u003ccode\u003ePurge the PIP cache\u003c/code\u003e. This will remove cached packages from the PIP cache, meaning it will have to download fresh copies.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAs we need to force the upgrade to the Python environment, the easiest way to do this will be to use \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e to \u003ccode\u003eDelete AllTalk's custom Python environment\u003c/code\u003e. This means it will have to rebuild the Python environment. \u003cstrong\u003eNote\u003c/strong\u003e, you may have to run this step twice, as it has to exit the current Python environment, then you have to re-load \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e and select \u003ccode\u003eDelete AllTalk's custom Python environment\u003c/code\u003e again.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eYou can now use \u003ccode\u003eatsetup\u003c/code\u003e to \u003ccode\u003eInstall AllTalk as a Standalone Application\u003c/code\u003e which will download fresh copies of everything and re-install the Python environment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOnce this is done you can check if CUDA is now working with the diagnostics or starting AllTalk and checking the model loads into CUDA.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 RuntimeError: PytorchStreamReader failed reading zip archive: failed finding central directory\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis error message is caused by the model being corrupted or damaged in some way. This error can occur if Huggingface, where the model is downloaded from, have an error (when the model is downloaded) or potentailly internet issues occuring while the model is downloaded on first start-up.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\nRuntimeError: PytorchStreamReader failed reading zip archive: failed finding central directory\n\nERROR: Application startup failed. Exiting.\n[AllTalk Startup] Warning TTS Subprocess has NOT started up yet, Will keep trying for 120 seconds maximum. Please wait.\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\nRuntimeError: PytorchStreamReader failed reading zip archive: failed finding central directory\n\nERROR: Application startup failed. Exiting.\n[AllTalk Startup] Warning TTS Subprocess has NOT started up yet, Will keep trying for 120 seconds maximum. Please wait.\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo resolve this, first look in your \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts/models/xttsv2_2.0.2\u003c/code\u003e (or whichever) model folder and confirm that the file sizes are correct.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/modelsfiles.jpg\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/modelsfiles.jpg\" alt=\"image\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can delete one or more suspect files and a factory fresh copy of that file or files will be downloaded on next start-up of AllTalk.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 RuntimeError: Found no NVIDIA driver on your system.\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis error message is caused by DeepSpeed being enabled when you do not have a Nvidia GPU. To resolve this, edit \u003ccode\u003econfignew.json\u003c/code\u003e and change \u003ccode\u003e\"deepspeed_activate\": true,\u003c/code\u003e to \u003ccode\u003e\"deepspeed_activate\": false,\u003c/code\u003e then restart AllTalk.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"  File \u0026quot;C:\\alltalk_tts\\alltalk_environment\\env\\Lib\\site-packages\\torch\\cuda\\__init__.py\u0026quot;, line 302, in _lazy_init\n    torch._C._cuda_init()\nRuntimeError: Found no NVIDIA driver on your system. Please check that you have an NVIDIA GPU and installed a driver from http://www.nvidia.com/Download/index.aspx\n\nERROR:    Application startup failed. Exiting.\n[AllTalk Startup] Warning TTS Subprocess has NOT started up yet, Will keep trying for 120 seconds maximum. DeepSpeed ops were compiled and installed.\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis error message is caused by having DeepSpeed enabled, but you have a version of DeepSpeed installed that was compiled for a different version of Python, PyTorch or CUDA (or any mix of those). You will need to start your Python environment and run \u003ccode\u003epip uninstall deepspeed\u003c/code\u003e to remove DeepSpeed from your Python environment and then install the correct version of DeepSpeed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"raise RuntimeError(\u0026quot;PyTorch version mismatch! DeepSpeed ops were compiled and installed \nRuntimeError: PyTorch version mismatch! DeepSpeed ops were compiled and installed with a different version than what is being used at runtime. Please re-install DeepSpeed or switch torch versions. Install torch version=2.1, Runtime torch version=2.2\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eraise RuntimeError(\"PyTorch version mismatch! DeepSpeed ops were compiled and installed \nRuntimeError: PyTorch version mismatch! DeepSpeed ops were compiled and installed with a different version than what is being used at runtime. Please re-install DeepSpeed or switch torch versions. Install torch version=2.1, Runtime torch version=2.2\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 Warning TTS Subprocess has NOT started up yet, Will keep trying for 120 seconds maximum. Please wait. It times out after 120 seconds.\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\tWhen the subprocess is starting 2x things are occurring:\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA)\u003c/strong\u003e Its trying to load the voice model into your graphics card VRAM (assuming you have a Nvidia Graphics card, otherwise its your system RAM)\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cstrong\u003eB)\u003c/strong\u003e Its trying to start up the mini-webserver and send the \"ready\" signal back to the main process.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBefore giving other possibilities a go, some people with \u003cstrong\u003eold machines\u003c/strong\u003e are finding their startup times are \u003cstrong\u003every\u003c/strong\u003e slow 2-3 minutes. Ive extended the allowed time within the script from 1 minute to 2 minutes. \u003cstrong\u003eIf you have an older machine\u003c/strong\u003e and wish to try extending this further, you can do so by editing \u003ccode\u003escript.py\u003c/code\u003e and changing \u003ccode\u003estartup_wait_time = 120\u003c/code\u003e (120 seconds, aka 2 minutes) at the top of the script.py file, to a larger value e.g \u003ccode\u003estartup_wait_time = 240\u003c/code\u003e (240 seconds aka 4 minutes).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e If you need to create a support ticket, please create a \u003ccode\u003ediagnostics.log\u003c/code\u003e report file to submit with a support request. Details on doing this are above.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOther possibilities for this issue are:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou are starting AllTalk in both your \u003ccode\u003eCMD FLAG.txt\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003esettings.yaml\u003c/code\u003e file. The \u003ccode\u003eCMD FLAG.txt\u003c/code\u003e you would have manually edited and the \u003ccode\u003esettings.yaml\u003c/code\u003e is the one you change and save in the \u003ccode\u003esession\u003c/code\u003e tab of text-generation-webui and you can \u003ccode\u003eSave UI defaults to settings.yaml\u003c/code\u003e. Please only have one of those two starting up AllTalk.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou are not starting text-generation-webui with its normal Python environment. Please start it with start_{your OS version} as detailed \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui#how-to-install\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e (\u003ccode\u003estart_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e,\u003ccode\u003e./start_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003estart_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003estart_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e) OR (\u003ccode\u003ecmd_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ecmd_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003ecmd_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e and then \u003ccode\u003epython server.py\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou have installed the wrong version of DeepSpeed on your system, for the wrong version of Python/Text-generation-webui. You can go to your text-generation-webui folder in a terminal/command prompt and run the correct cmd version for your OS e.g. (\u003ccode\u003ecmd_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ecmd_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003ecmd_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e) and then you can type \u003ccode\u003epip uninstall deepspeed\u003c/code\u003e then try loading it again. If that works, please see here for the correct instructions for installing DeepSpeed \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts#-deepspeed-installation-options\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou have an old version of text-generation-webui (pre Dec 2023) I have not tested on older versions of text-generation-webui, so cannot confirm viability on older versions. For instructions on updating the text-generation-webui, please look \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui#how-to-install\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eupdate_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eupdate_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eupdate_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e, or \u003ccode\u003eupdate_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou already have something running on port 7851 on your computer, so the mini-webserver cant start on that port. You can change this port number by editing the \u003ccode\u003econfignew.json\u003c/code\u003e file and changing \u003ccode\u003e\"port_number\": \"7851\"\u003c/code\u003e to \u003ccode\u003e\"port_number\": \"7602\"\u003c/code\u003e or any port number you wish that isn’t reserved. Only change the number and save the file, do not change the formatting of the document. This will at least discount that you have something else clashing on the same port number.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou have antivirus/firewalling that is blocking that port from being accessed. If you had to do something to allow text-generation-webui through your antivirus/firewall, you will have to do that for this too.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou have quite old graphics drivers and may need to update them.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSomething within text-generation-webui is not playing nicely for some reason. You can go to your text-generation-webui folder in a terminal/command prompt and run the correct cmd version for your OS e.g. (\u003ccode\u003ecmd_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ecmd_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003ecmd_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e) and then you can type \u003ccode\u003epython extensions\\alltalk_tts\\script.py\u003c/code\u003e and see if AllTalk starts up correctly. If it does then something else is interfering.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSomething else is already loaded into your VRAM or there is a crashed python process. Either check your task manager for erroneous Python processes or restart your machine and try again.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou are running DeepSpeed on a Linux machine and although you are starting with \u003ccode\u003e./start_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e AllTalk is failing there on starting. This is because text-generation-webui will overwrite some environment variables when it loads its python environment. To see if this is the problem, from a terminal go into your text-generation-webui folder and \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e then set your environment variable again e.g. \u003ccode\u003eexport CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda\u003c/code\u003e (this may vary depending on your OS, but this is the standard one for Linux, and assuming you have installed the CUDA toolkit), then \u003ccode\u003epython server.py\u003c/code\u003e and see if it starts up. If you want to edit the environment permanently you can do so, I have not managed to write full instructions yet, but here is the conda guide \u003ca href=\"https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-environments.html#set-env-vars\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou have built yourself a custom Python environment and something is funky with it. This is very hard to diagnose as its not a standard environment. You may want to updating text-generation-webui and re installing its requirements file (whichever one you use that comes down with text-generation-webui).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 I have multiple GPU's and I have problems running Finetuning\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFinetuning pulls in various other scripts and some of those scripts can have issues with multiple Nvidia GPU's being present. Until the people that created those other scripts fix up their code, there is a workaround to temporarily tell your system to only use the 1x of your Nvidia GPU's. To do this:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWindows\u003c/strong\u003e - You will start the script with \u003ccode\u003eset CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 \u0026amp;\u0026amp; python finetune.py\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nAfter you have completed training, you can reset back with \u003ccode\u003eset CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLinux\u003c/strong\u003e - You will start the script with \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python finetune.py\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nAfter you have completed training, you can reset back with \u003ccode\u003eunset CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRebooting your system will also unset this. The setting is only applied temporarily.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDepending on which of your Nvidia GPU's is the more powerful one, you can change the \u003ccode\u003e0\u003c/code\u003e to \u003ccode\u003e1\u003c/code\u003e or whichever of your GPU's is the most powerful.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 Firefox - Streaming Audio doesnt work on Firefox\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis is a long standing issue with Mozilla \u0026amp; Firefox and one I am unable to resolve as Mozilla have not resolved the issue with Firefox. The solution is to use another web browser if you want to use Streaming audio. For details of my prior invesitigation please look at this \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/143\" data-hovercard-type=\"issue\" data-hovercard-url=\"/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/143/hovercard\"\u003eticket\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 Hindi Support - Not working or issues\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eHindi support does not officially exist according to Coqui. Ive added a limited Hindi support at this time, however, It only works with API TTS method and Im sure there will be issues. \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178\" data-hovercard-type=\"issue\" data-hovercard-url=\"/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178/hovercard\"\u003eticket\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eApplication Specific Issues\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-application-specific-issues\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Application Specific Issues\" href=\"#application-specific-issues\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 SillyTavern - I changed my IP address and now SillyTavern wont connect with AllTalk\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nSillyTavern checks the IP address when loading extensions, saving the IP to its configuration only if the check succeeds. For whatever reason, SillyTavern's checks dont always allow changing its IP address a second time.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo manually change the IP address:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to the SillyTavern Public folder located at \u003ccode\u003e/sillytavern/public/\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOpen the \u003ccode\u003esettings.json\u003c/code\u003e file.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLook for the AllTalk section and find the \u003ccode\u003eprovider_endpoint\u003c/code\u003e entry.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eReplace \u003ccode\u003elocalhost\u003c/code\u003e with your desired IP address, for example, \u003ccode\u003e192.168.1.64\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/35898566/304162975-144e4ac4-87dc-4a2b-8a73-39314abed1ca.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..Gd3AqY0zuqIrNYSaV6_mcoHu8Ndd8oSNCSs4SJI-6KI\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/35898566/304162975-144e4ac4-87dc-4a2b-8a73-39314abed1ca.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..Gd3AqY0zuqIrNYSaV6_mcoHu8Ndd8oSNCSs4SJI-6KI\" alt=\"image\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eTTS Generation Issues \u0026amp; Questions\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-tts-generation-issues--questions\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: TTS Generation Issues \u0026amp; Questions\" href=\"#tts-generation-issues--questions\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 XTTS - Does the XTTS AI Model Support Emotion Control or Singing?\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNo, the XTTS AI model does not currently support direct control over emotions or singing capabilities. While XTTS infuses generated speech with a degree of emotional intonation based on the context of the text, users cannot explicitly control this aspect. It's worth noting that regenerating the same line of TTS may yield slightly different emotional inflections, but there is no way to directly control it with XTTS.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003e🟨 XTTS - Skips, repeats or pronunciation Issues\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFirstly, it's important to clarify that the development and maintenance of the XTTS AI models and core scripts are handled by \u003ca href=\"https://docs.coqui.ai/en/latest/index.html\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eCoqui\u003c/a\u003e, with additional scripts and libraries from entities like \u003ca href=\"https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/en/index\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehuggingface\u003c/a\u003e among many other Python scripts and libraries used by AllTalk.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAllTalk is designed to be a straightforward interface that simplifies setup and interaction with AI TTS models like XTTS. Currently, AllTalk supports the XTTS model, with plans to include more models in the future. Please understand that the deep inner workings of XTTS, including reasons why it may skip, repeat, or mispronounce, along with 3rd party scripts and libraries utilized, are ultimately outside my control.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAlthough I ensure the text processed through AllTalk is accurately relayed to the XTTS model speech generation process, and I have aimed to mitigate as many issues as much as possible; skips, repeats and bad pronounciation can still occur.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCertain aspects I have not been able to investigate due to my own time limitations, are:\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe impact of DeepSpeed on TTS quality. Is this more likely to cause skips or repetition?\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eComparative performance between different XTTS model versions (e.g., 2.0.3 vs. 2.0.2) regarding audio quality and consistency.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFrom my experience and anecdotally gained knowledge:\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLower quality voice samples tend to produce more anomalies in generated speech.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eModel finetuning with high-quality voice samples significantly reduces such issues, enhancing overall speech quality.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUnused/Excessive punctuation causes issues e.g. asterisks \u003ccode\u003e*\u003c/code\u003e, hashes \u003ccode\u003e#\u003c/code\u003e, brackets \u003ccode\u003e(\u003c/code\u003e \u003ccode\u003e)\u003c/code\u003e etc. Many of these AllTalk will filter out.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSo for example, the \u003ccode\u003efemale_01.wav\u003c/code\u003e file that is provided with AllTalk is a studio quality voice sample, which the XTTS model was trained on. Typically you will find it unlikely that anomolies occur with TTS generation when using this voice sample. Hence good quality samples and finetuning, generally improve results with XTTS.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you wish to try out the XTTS version 2.0.3 model and see if it works better, you can download it from \u003ca href=\"https://huggingface.co/coqui/XTTS-v2/tree/v2.0.3\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e, replacing all the files within your \u003ccode\u003e/alltalk_tts/models/xttsv2_2.0.2\u003c/code\u003e folder. This is on my list to both test version 2.0.3 more, but also build a more flexible TTS models downloader, that will not only accomdating other XTTS models, but also other TTS engines. If you try the XTTS version 2.0.3 model and gleen any insights, please let me know.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Finetuning a model\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--finetuning-a-model\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Finetuning a model\" href=\"#-finetuning-a-model\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you have a voice that the model doesnt quite reproduce correctly, or indeed you just want to improve the reproduced voice, then finetuning is a way to train your \"XTTSv2 local\" model \u003cstrong\u003e(stored in \u003ccode\u003e/alltalk_tts/models/xxxxx/\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/strong\u003e on a specific voice. For this you will need:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAn Nvidia graphics card. (Please see the help section \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/edit/main/README.md#performance-and-compatibility-issues\"\u003enote\u003c/a\u003e if you have multiple Nvidia GPU's). Preferably 12GB+ VRAM on Windows. Minimum 16GB VRAM on Linux.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e18GB of disk space free (most of this is used temporarily)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAt least 2 minutes of good quality speech from your chosen speaker in mp3, wav or flacc format, in one or more files (have tested as far as 20 minutes worth of audio).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAs a side note, many people seem to think that the Whisper v2 model (used on Step 1) is giving better results at generating training datasets, so you may prefer to try that, as opposed to the Whisper 3 model.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ How will this work/How complicated is it?\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--how-will-this-workhow-complicated-is-it\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ How will this work/How complicated is it?\" href=\"#-how-will-this-workhow-complicated-is-it\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEverything has been done to make this as simple as possible. At its simplest, you can literally just download a large chunk of audio from an interview, and tell the finetuning to strip through it, find spoken parts and build your dataset. You can literally click 4 buttons, then copy a few files and you are done. At it's more complicated end you will clean up the audio a little beforehand, but its still only 4x buttons and copying a few files.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ The audio you will use\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--the-audio-you-will-use\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ The audio you will use\" href=\"#-the-audio-you-will-use\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI would suggest that if its in an interview format, you cut out the interviewer speaking in audacity or your chosen audio editing package. You dont have to worry about being perfect with your cuts, the finetuning Step 1 will go and find spoken audio and cut it out for you. Is there is music over the spoken parts, for best quality you would cut out those parts, though its not 100% necessary. As always, try to avoid bad quality audio with noises in it (humming sounds, hiss etc). You can try something like \u003ca href=\"https://audioenhancer.ai/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eAudioenhancer\u003c/a\u003e to try clean up noisier audio. There is no need to down-sample any of the audio, all of that is handled for you. Just give the finetuning some good quality audio to work with.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Can I Finetune a model more than once on more than one voice\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--can-i-finetune-a-model-more-than-once-on-more-than-one-voice\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Can I Finetune a model more than once on more than one voice\" href=\"#-can-i-finetune-a-model-more-than-once-on-more-than-one-voice\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes you can. You would do these as multiple finetuning's, but its absolutely possible and fine to do. Finetuning the XTTS model does not restrict it to only being able to reproduce that 1x voice you trained it on. Finetuning is generally nuding the model in a direction to learn the ability to sound a bit more like a voice its not heard before.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ A note about anonymous training Telemetry information \u0026amp; disabling it\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--a-note-about-anonymous-training-telemetry-information--disabling-it\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ A note about anonymous training Telemetry information \u0026amp; disabling it\" href=\"#-a-note-about-anonymous-training-telemetry-information--disabling-it\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePortions of Coqui's TTS trainer scripts gather anonymous training information which you can disable. Their statement on this is listed \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/coqui-ai/Trainer?tab=readme-ov-file#anonymized-telemetry\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e. If you start AllTalk Finetuning with \u003ccode\u003estart_finetuning.bat\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e./start_finetuning.sh\u003c/code\u003e telemetry will be disabled. If you manually want to disable it, please expand the below:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eManually disable telemetry\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBefore starting finetuning, run the following in your terminal/command prompt:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOn Windows by typing \u003ccode\u003eset TRAINER_TELEMETRY=0\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOn Linux \u0026amp; Mac by typing \u003ccode\u003eexport TRAINER_TELEMETRY=0\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBefore you start \u003ccode\u003efinetune.py\u003c/code\u003e. You will now be able to finetune offline and no anonymous training data will be sent.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Prerequisites for Fine-tuning with Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit 11.8\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--prerequisites-for-fine-tuning-with-nvidia-cuda-development-toolkit-118\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Prerequisites for Fine-tuning with Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit 11.8\" href=\"#-prerequisites-for-fine-tuning-with-nvidia-cuda-development-toolkit-118\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAll the requirements for Finetuning will be installed by using the atsetup utility and installing your correct requirements (Standalone or for Text-generation-webui). The legacy manual instructions are stored below, however these shouldnt be required.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\t\u003csummary\u003eLegacy manual instructions for installing Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit 11.8\u003c/summary\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n- To perform fine-tuning, a specific portion of the **Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit v11.8** must be installed. This is crucial for step 1 of fine-tuning. The objective is to minimize the installation footprint by installing only the essential components.\n- The **Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit v11.8** operates independently from your graphics card drivers and the CUDA version utilized by your Python environment.\n- This installation process aims to keep the download and install size as minimal as possible, however a full install of the tookit requires 3GB's of disk space.\n- When running Finetuning it will require upto 20GB's of temporary disk space, so please ensure you have this space available and preferably use a SSD or NVME drive.\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDownload the Toolkit\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eObtain the \u003cstrong\u003enetwork install\u003c/strong\u003e version of the Nvidia CUDA Development Toolkit 11.8 from \u003ca href=\"https://developer.nvidia.com/cuda-11-8-0-download-archive\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eNvidia's Archive\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRun the Installer\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eChoose \u003cstrong\u003eCustom (Advanced)\u003c/strong\u003e installation.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDeselect all options initially.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSelect the following components:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eCUDA\u003c/code\u003e \u0026gt; \u003ccode\u003eDevelopment\u003c/code\u003e \u0026gt; \u003ccode\u003eCompiler\u003c/code\u003e \u0026gt; \u003ccode\u003envcc\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eCUDA\u003c/code\u003e \u0026gt; \u003ccode\u003eDevelopment\u003c/code\u003e \u0026gt; \u003ccode\u003eLibraries\u003c/code\u003e \u0026gt; \u003ccode\u003eCUBLAS\u003c/code\u003e (\u003cstrong\u003eboth\u003c/strong\u003e development and runtime)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eConfigure Environment Search Path\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIt's essential that \u003ccode\u003envcc\u003c/code\u003e and CUDA 11.8 library files are discoverable in your environment's search path. Adjustments can be reverted post-fine-tuning if desired.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFor Windows\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEdit the \u003ccode\u003ePath\u003c/code\u003e environment variable to include \u003ccode\u003eC:\\Program Files\\NVIDIA GPU Computing Toolkit\\CUDA\\v11.8\\bin\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAdd \u003ccode\u003eCUDA_HOME\u003c/code\u003e and set its path to \u003ccode\u003eC:\\Program Files\\NVIDIA GPU Computing Toolkit\\CUDA\\v11.8.\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFor Linux\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe path may vary by Linux distribution. Here's a generic setup:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport PATH=${CUDA_HOME}/bin:${PATH}\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${CUDA_HOME}/lib64:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eConsider adding these to your \u003ccode\u003e~/.bashrc\u003c/code\u003e for permanence, or apply temporarily for the current session by running the above commands each time you start your Python environment.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNote\u003c/strong\u003e: If using Text-generation-webui, its best to set these temporarily.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVerify Installation\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOpen a \u003cstrong\u003enew\u003c/strong\u003e terminal/command prompt to refresh the search paths.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIn a terminal or command prompt, execute \u003ccode\u003envcc --version\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSuccess is indicated by a response of \u003ccode\u003eCuda compilation tools, release 11.8.\u003c/code\u003e Specifically, ensure it is version 11.8.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTroubleshooting\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf the correct version isn't reported, recheck your environment path settings for accuracy and potential conflicts with other CUDA versions.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eAdditional Note on Torch and Torchaudio:\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-additional-note-on-torch-and-torchaudio\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Additional Note on Torch and Torchaudio:\" href=\"#additional-note-on-torch-and-torchaudio\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEnsure Torch and Torchaudio are CUDA-enabled (any version), which is separate from the CUDA Toolkit installation. CUDA 11.8 corresponds to \u003ccode\u003ecu118\u003c/code\u003e and CUDA 12.1 to \u003ccode\u003ecu121\u003c/code\u003e in AllTalk diagnostics.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFailure to install CUDA for Torch and Torchaudio will result in Step 2 of fine-tuning failing. These requirements are distinct from the CUDA Toolkit installation, so avoid conflating the two.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Starting Fine-tuning\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--starting-fine-tuning\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Starting Fine-tuning\" href=\"#-starting-fine-tuning\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNOTE:\u003c/strong\u003e Ensure AllTalk has been launched at least once after any updates to download necessary files for fine-tuning.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eClose Resource-Intensive Applications\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTerminate any applications that are using your GPU/VRAM to ensure enough resources for fine-tuning.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOrganize Voice Samples\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ePlace your audio samples into the following directory:\n\u003ccode\u003e/alltalk_tts/finetune/put-voice-samples-in-here/\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDepending on your setup (Text-generation-webui or Standalone AllTalk), the steps to start the Python environment vary:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFor Standalone AllTalk Users\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to the \u003ccode\u003ealltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e folder:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eStart the Python environment:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003estart_finetune.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./start_finetune.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFor Text-generation-webui Users\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNavigate to the Text-generation-webui directory:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd text-generation-webui\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eStart the Python environment suitable for your OS:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows: \u003ccode\u003ecmd_windows.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLinux: \u003ccode\u003e./cmd_linux.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emacOS: \u003ccode\u003ecmd_macos.sh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux): \u003ccode\u003ecmd_wsl.bat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMove into the AllTalk directory:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecd extensions/alltalk_tts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLinux\u003c/strong\u003e users only need to run this command:\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\" export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=`python3 -c 'import os; import nvidia.cublas.lib; import nvidia.cudnn.lib; print(os.path.dirname(nvidia.cublas.lib.__file__) + \u0026quot;:\u0026quot; + os.path.dirname(nvidia.cudnn.lib.__file__))'`\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=`python3 -c 'import os; import nvidia.cublas.lib; import nvidia.cudnn.lib; print(os.path.dirname(nvidia.cublas.lib.__file__) + \":\" + os.path.dirname(nvidia.cudnn.lib.__file__))'`\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eStart the fine-tuning process with the command:\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003epython finetune.py\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're unfamiliar with Python environments and wish to learn more, consider reviewing \u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding Python Environments Simplified\u003c/strong\u003e in the Help section.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003col start=\"3\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePre-Flight Checklist\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGo through the pre-flight checklist to ensure readiness. Address any issues flagged as \"Fail\".\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePost Fine-tuning Actions\u003c/strong\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUpon completing fine-tuning, the final tab will guide you on managing your files and relocating your newly trained model to the appropriate directory.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThese steps guide you through the initial preparations, starting the Python environment based on your setup, and the fine-tuning process itself. Ensure all prerequisites are met to facilitate a smooth fine-tuning experience.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ How many Epochs etc is the right amount?\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--how-many-epochs-etc-is-the-right-amount\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ How many Epochs etc is the right amount?\" href=\"#-how-many-epochs-etc-is-the-right-amount\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn finetuning the suggested/recommended amount of epochs, batch size, evaluation percent etc is already set. However, there is no absolutely correct answer to what the settings should be, it all depends on what you are doing.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf you just want to train a normal human voice that is in an existing language, for most people’s needs, the base settings would work fine. You may choose to increase the epochs up to maybe 20, or run a second round of training if needed.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf you were training an entirely new language, you would need a huge amount of training data and it requires around 1000 epochs (based on things I can find around the internet of people who tried this).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf you are training a cartoon style voice in an existing language, it may need well upwards of 40 epochs until it can reproduce that voice with some success.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThere are no absolute correct settings, as there are too many variables, ranging from the amount of samples you are using (5 minutes worth? 4 hours worth? etc), if they are similar samples to what the AI model already understands, so on and so forth. Coqui whom originally trained the model usually say something along the lines of, once you’ve trained it X amount, if it sounds good then you are done and if it doesn’t, train it more.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Evaluation Data Percentage\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--evaluation-data-percentage\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Evaluation Data Percentage\" href=\"#-evaluation-data-percentage\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn the process of finetuning, it's crucial to balance the data used for training the model against the data reserved for evaluating its performance. Typically, a portion of the dataset is set aside as an 'evaluation set' to assess the model's capabilities in dealing with unseen data. On Step 1 of finetuning you have the option to adjust this evaluation data percentage, offering more control over your model training process.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cstrong\u003eWhy Adjust the Evaluation Percentage?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nAdjusting the evaluation percentage \u003cstrong\u003ecan\u003c/strong\u003e be beneficial in scenarios with limited voice samples. When dealing with a smaller dataset, allocating a slightly larger portion to training could enhance the model's ability to learn from these scarce samples. Conversely, with abundant data, a higher evaluation percentage might be more appropriate to rigorously test the model's performance. There are currently no absolutely optimal split percentages as it varies by dataset.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDefault Setting:\u003c/strong\u003e The default evaluation percentage is set at 15%, which is a balanced choice for most datasets.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAdjustable Range:\u003c/strong\u003e Users can now adjust this percentage, but it’s generally recommend keeping it between 5% and 30%.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLower Bound:\u003c/strong\u003e A minimum of 5% ensures that there's enough data to evaluate model performance.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUpper Bound:\u003c/strong\u003e Its suggested not exceeding 30% for evaluation to avoid limiting the amount of data available for training.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUnderstanding the Impact:\u003c/strong\u003e Before adjusting this setting, it's important to understand its impact on model training and evaluation. Incorrect adjustments can lead to suboptimal model performance.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGradual Adjustments:\u003c/strong\u003e For those unfamiliar with the process, we recommend reading up on training data and training sets, then making small, incremental changes and observing their effects.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eData Quality:\u003c/strong\u003e Regardless of the split, the quality of the audio data is paramount. Ensure that your datasets are built from good quality audio with enough data within them.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Using a Finetuned model in Text-generation-webui\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--using-a-finetuned-model-in-text-generation-webui\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Using a Finetuned model in Text-generation-webui\" href=\"#-using-a-finetuned-model-in-text-generation-webui\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAt the end of the finetune process, you will have an option to \u003ccode\u003eCompact and move model to /trainedmodel/\u003c/code\u003e this will compact the raw training file and move it to \u003ccode\u003e/model/trainedmodel/\u003c/code\u003e. When AllTalk starts up within Text-generation-webui, if it finds a model in this location a new loader will appear in the interface for \u003ccode\u003eXTTSv2 FT\u003c/code\u003e and you can use this to load your finetuned model. \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBe careful\u003c/strong\u003e not to train a new model from the base model, then overwrite your current \u003ccode\u003e/model/trainedmodel/\u003c/code\u003e \u003cstrong\u003eif\u003c/strong\u003e you want a seperately trained model. This is why there is an \u003ccode\u003eOPTION B\u003c/code\u003e to move your just trained model to \u003ccode\u003e/models/lastfinetuned/\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Training one model with multiple voices\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--training-one-model-with-multiple-voices\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Training one model with multiple voices\" href=\"#-training-one-model-with-multiple-voices\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAt the end of the finetune process, you will have an option to \u003ccode\u003eCompact and move model to /trainedmodel/\u003c/code\u003e this will compact the raw training file and move it to \u003ccode\u003e/model/trainedmodel/\u003c/code\u003e. This model will become available when you start up finetuning. You will have a choice to train the Base Model or the \u003ccode\u003eExisting finetuned model\u003c/code\u003e (which is the one in \u003ccode\u003e/model/trainedmodel/\u003c/code\u003e). So you can use this to keep further training this model with additional voices, then copying it back to \u003ccode\u003e/model/trainedmodel/\u003c/code\u003e at the end of training.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ Do I need to keep the raw training data/model?\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--do-i-need-to-keep-the-raw-training-datamodel\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⚫ Do I need to keep the raw training data/model?\" href=\"#-do-i-need-to-keep-the-raw-training-datamodel\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you've compacted and moved your model, its highly unlikely you would want to keep that data, however the choice is there to keep it if you wish. It will be between 5-10GB in size, so most people will want to delete it.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚫ I have deeper questions about training the XTTS model, where can I find more information?\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca
