id=\"user-content--alltalk-tts-generator\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ AllTalk TTS Generator\" href=\"#-alltalk-tts-generator\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAllTalk TTS Generator is the solution for converting large volumes of text into speech using the voice of your choice. Whether you're creating audio content or just want to hear text read aloud, the TTS Generator is equipped to handle it all efficiently. Please see here for a quick \u003ca href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hunvXn0mLzc\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003edemo\u003c/a\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003eThe link to open the TTS generator can be found on the built-in Settings and Documentation page.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDeepSpeed\u003c/strong\u003e is \u003cstrong\u003ehighly\u003c/strong\u003e recommended to speed up generation. \u003cstrong\u003eLow VRAM\u003c/strong\u003e would be best turned off and your LLM model unloaded from your GPU VRAM (unload your model). \u003cstrong\u003eNo Playback\u003c/strong\u003e will reduce memory overhead on very large generations (15,000 words or more). Splitting \u003cstrong\u003eExport to Wav\u003c/strong\u003e into smaller groups will also reduce memory overhead at the point of exporting your wav files (so good for low memory systems).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Estimated Throughput\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--estimated-throughput\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Estimated Throughput\" href=\"#-estimated-throughput\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis will vary by system for a multitude of reasons, however, while generating a 58,000 word document to TTS, with DeepSpeed enabled, LowVram disabled, splitting size 2 and on an Nvidia RTX 4070, throughput was around 1,000 words per minute. Meaning, this took 1 hour to generate the TTS. Exporting to combined wavs took about 2-3 minutes total.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Quick Start\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--quick-start\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Quick Start\" href=\"#-quick-start\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eText Input:\u003c/strong\u003e Enter the text you wish to convert into speech in the 'Text Input' box.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGenerate TTS:\u003c/strong\u003e Hit this to start the text-to-speech conversion.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePause/Resume:\u003c/strong\u003e Used to pause and resume the playback of the initial generation of wavs or the stream.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStop Playback:\u003c/strong\u003e This will stop the current audio playing back. It does not stop the text from being generated however.\nOnce you have sent text off to be generated, either as a stream or wav file generation, the TTS server will remain busy until this process has competed. As such, think carefully as to how much you want to send to the server.\nIf you are generating wav files and populating the queue, you can generate one lot of text to speech, then input your next lot of text and it will continue adding to the list.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Customization and Preferences\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--customization-and-preferences\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Customization and Preferences\" href=\"#-customization-and-preferences\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCharacter Voice:\u003c/strong\u003e Choose the voice that will read your text.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLanguage:\u003c/strong\u003e Select the language of your text.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eChunk Sizes:\u003c/strong\u003e Decide the size of text chunks for generation. Smaller sizes are recommended for better TTS quality.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" your visual comfort.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWord Count and Generation Queue:\u003c/strong\u003e Keep track of the word count and the generation progress.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ TTS Generation Modes\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--tts-generation-modes\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ TTS Generation Modes\" href=\"#-tts-generation-modes\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWav Chunks:\u003c/strong\u003e Perfect for creating audio books, or anything you want to keep long term. Breaks down your text into manageable wav files and queues them up. Generation begins automatically, and playback will start after a few chunks have been prepared ahead. You can set the volume to 0 if you don’t want to hear playback. With Wav chunks, you can edit and/or regenerate portions of the TTS as needed.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStreaming:\u003c/strong\u003e For immediate playback without the ability to save. Ideal for on-the-fly speech generation and listening. This will not generate wav files and it will play back through your browser. You cannot stop the server generating the TTS once it has been sent.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nWith wav chunks you can either playback “In Browser” which is the web page you are on, or “On Server” which is through the console/terminal where AllTalk is running from, or \"No Playback\". Only generation “In Browser” can play back smoothly and populate the Generated TTS List. Setting the Volume will affect the volume level played back both “In Browser” and “On Server”.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nFor generating \u003cstrong\u003elarge amounts of TTS\u003c/strong\u003e, it's recommended to select the \u003cstrong\u003eNo Playback\u003c/strong\u003e option. This setting minimizes the memory usage in your web browser by avoiding the loading and playing of audio files directly within the browser, which is particularly beneficial for handling extensive audio generations. The definition of large will vary depending on your system RAM availability (will update when I have more information as to guidelines). Once the audio is generated, you can export your list to JSON (for safety) and use the \u003cstrong\u003ePlay List\u003c/strong\u003e option to play back your audio.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Playback and List Management\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--playback-and-list-management\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Playback and List Management\" href=\"#-playback-and-list-management\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePlayback Controls:\u003c/strong\u003e Utilize 'Play List' to start from the beginning or 'Stop Playback' to halt at any time.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCustom Start:\u003c/strong\u003e Jump into your list at a specific ID to hear a particular section.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRegeneration and Editing:\u003c/strong\u003e If a chunk isn't quite right, you can opt to regenerate it or edit the text directly. Click off the text to save changes and hit regenerate for the specific line.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eExport/Import List:\u003c/strong\u003e Save your TTS list as a JSON file or import one. Note: Existing wav files are needed for playback. Exporting is handy if you want to take your files away into another program and have a list of which wav is which, or if you keep your audio files, but want to come back at a later date, edit one or two lines, regenerate the speech and re-combine the wav’s into one new long wav.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Exporting Your Audio\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--exporting-your-audio\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Exporting Your Audio\" href=\"#-exporting-your-audio\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eExport to WAV:\u003c/strong\u003e Combine all generated TTS from the list, into one single WAV file for easy download and distribution. Its always recommended to export your list to a JSON before exporting, so that you have a backup, should something go wrong. You can simply re-import the list and try exporting again.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003eWhen exporting, there is a file size limit of 1GB and as such you have the option to choose how many files to include in each block of audio exported. 600 is just on the limit of 1GB, depending on the average file size, so 500 or less is a good amount to work with. You can combine the generated files after if you wish, in Audacity or similar.\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003eAdditionally, lower export batches will lower the memory requirements, so if your system is low on memory (maybe 8 or 16GB system), you can use smaller export batches to keep the memory requirement down.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Exporting Subtitles (SRT file)\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--exporting-subtitles-srt-file\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Exporting Subtitles (SRT file)\" href=\"#-exporting-subtitles-srt-file\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 This will scan through all wav files in your list and generate a subtitles file that will match your exported wav file.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Analyzing generated TTS for errors\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--analyzing-generated-tts-for-errors\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Analyzing generated TTS for errors\" href=\"#-analyzing-generated-tts-for-errors\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAnalyze TTS:\u003c/strong\u003e This will scan through all wav files comparing each ID's orignal text with the TTS generated for that ID and then flag up inconsistences. Its important to understand this is a \u003cstrong\u003ebest effort\u003c/strong\u003e process and \u003cstrong\u003enot 100% perfect\u003c/strong\u003e, for example:\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eYour text may have the word \u003ccode\u003etheir\u003c/code\u003e and the automated routine that listens to your generated TTS interprets the word as \u003ccode\u003ethere\u003c/code\u003e, aka a spelling difference.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eYour text may have \u003ccode\u003eExamples are:\u003c/code\u003e (note the colon) and the automated routine that listens to your generated TTS interprets the word as \"Examples are` (note no colon as you cannot sound out a colon in TTS), aka a punctuation difference.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eYour text may have \u003ccode\u003eThere are 100 items\u003c/code\u003e and the automated routine that listens to your generated TTS interprets the word as \u003ccode\u003eThere are one hundred items\u003c/code\u003e, aka numbers vs the number written out in words.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThere will be other examples such as double quotes. As I say, please remember this is a \u003cstrong\u003ebest effort\u003c/strong\u003e to help you identify issues.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAs such, there is a \u003ccode\u003e% Accuracy\u003c/code\u003e setting. This uses a couple of methods to try find things that are similar e.g. taking the \u003ccode\u003etheir\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003ethere\u003c/code\u003e example from above, it would identify that they both sound the same, so even if the text says \u003ccode\u003etheir\u003c/code\u003e and the AI listening to the generated TTS interprets the word as \u003ccode\u003ethere\u003c/code\u003e, it will realise that both sound the same/are similar so there is no need to flag that as an error. However, there are limits to this and some things may slip through or get picked up when you would prefer them not to be flagged.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe higher the accuracy you choose, the more things it will flag up, however you may get more unwanted detections. The lower the less detections. Based on my few tests, accuracy settings between 96 to 98 seem to generally give the best results. However, I would highly recommend you test out a small 10-20 line text and test out the \u003cstrong\u003eAnalyze TTS\u003c/strong\u003e button to get a feel for how it responds to different settings, as well as things it flags up.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou will be able to see the ID's and Text (orignal and as interpreted) by looking at the terminal/command prompt window.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Analyze TTS feature uses the Whisper Larger-v2 AI engine, which will download on first use if necessary. This will require about 2.5GB's of disk space and could take a few minutes to download, depending on your internet connection.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can use this feature on systems that do not have an Nvidia GPU, however, unless you have a very powerful CPU, expect it to be slow.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⬜ Tricks to get the model to say things correctly\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--tricks-to-get-the-model-to-say-things-correctly\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⬜ Tricks to get the model to say things correctly\" href=\"#-tricks-to-get-the-model-to-say-things-correctly\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSometimes the AI model won’t say something the way that you want it to. It could be because it’s a new word, an acronym or just something it’s not good at for whatever reason. There are some tricks you can use to improve the chances of it saying something correctly.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAdding pauses\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nYou can use semi-colons \";\" and colons \":\" to create a pause, similar to a period \".\" which can be helpful with some splitting issues.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAcronyms\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\nNot all acronyms are going to be pronounced correctly. Let’s work with the word \u003ccode\u003eChatGPT\u003c/code\u003e. We know it is pronounced \u003ccode\u003e\"Chat G P T\"\u003c/code\u003e but when presented to the model, it doesn’t know how to break it down correctly. So, there are a few ways we could get it to break out \"Chat\" and the G P and T. e.g.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eChat G P T.\u003c/code\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eChat G,P,T.\u003c/code\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eChat G.P.T.\u003c/code\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eChat G-P-T.\u003c/code\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eChat gee pee tea\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAll bar the last one are using ways within the English language to split out \"Chat\" into one word being pronounced and then split the G, P and T into individual letters. The final example, which is to use Phonetics will sound perfectly fine, but clearly would look wrong as far as human readable text goes. The phonetics method is very useful in edge cases where pronunciation difficult.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" characters, which you can control with the Chunk sizes.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGenerated audio can be played back from the list, which also highlights the currently playing chunk.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe TTS Generator remembers your settings, so you can pick up where you left off even after refreshing the Standard TTS speech Example (standard text) generating a time-stamped file\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecurl -X POST \"http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/tts-generate\" -d \"text_input=All of this is text spoken by the character. This is text not inside quotes, though that doesnt matter in the slightest\" -d \"text_filtering=standard\" -d \"character_voice_gen=female_01.wav\" -d \"narrator_enabled=false\" -d \"narrator_voice_gen=male_01.wav\" -d \"text_not_inside=character\" -d \"language=en\" -d \"output_file_name=myoutputfile\" -d \"output_file_timestamp=true\" -d \"autoplay=true\" -d \"autoplay_volume=0.8\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNarrator Example (standard text) generating a time-stamped file\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecurl -X POST \"http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/tts-generate\" -d \"text_input=*This is text spoken by the narrator* \\\"This is text spoken by the character\\\". This is text not inside quotes.\" -d \"text_filtering=standard\" -d \"character_voice_gen=female_01.wav\" -d \"narrator_enabled=true\" -d \"narrator_voice_gen=male_01.wav\" -d \"text_not_inside=character\" -d \"language=en\" -d \"output_file_name=myoutputfile\" -d \"output_file_timestamp=true\" -d \"autoplay=true\" -d \"autoplay_volume=0.8\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNote that if your text that needs to be generated contains double quotes you will need to escape them with \u003ccode\u003e\\\"\u003c/code\u003e (Please see the narrator example).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 Request Parameters\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--request-parameters\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟠 Request Parameters\" href=\"#-request-parameters\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003etext_input\u003c/strong\u003e: The text you want the TTS engine to produce. Use escaped double quotes for character speech and asterisks for narrator speech if using the narrator function. Example:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"text_input=*This is text spoken by the narrator* \\\"This is text spoken by the character\\\". This is text not inside quotes.\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003etext_filtering\u003c/strong\u003e: Filter for text. Options:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003enone\u003c/strong\u003e No filtering. Whatever is sent will go over to the TTS engine as raw text, which may result in some odd sounds with some special characters.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003estandard\u003c/strong\u003e Human-readable text and a basic level of filtering, just to clean up some special characters.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ehtml\u003c/strong\u003e HTML content. Where you are using HTML entity's like \"\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"text_filtering=none\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003e-d \"text_filtering=standard\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003e-d \"text_filtering=html\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eExample:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStandard Example\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003e*This is text spoken by the narrator* \"This is text spoken by the character\" This is text not inside quotes.\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHTML Example\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003e\u0026amp;ast;This is text spoken by the narrator\u0026amp;ast; \u0026amp;quot;This is text spoken by the character\u0026amp;quot; This is text not inside quotes.\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNone\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003eWill just pass whatever characters/text you send at it.\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003echaracter_voice_gen\u003c/strong\u003e: The WAV file name for the character's voice.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"character_voice_gen=female_01.wav\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003enarrator_enabled\u003c/strong\u003e: Enable or disable the narrator function. If true, minimum text filtering is set to standard. Anything between double quotes is considered the character's speech, and anything between asterisks is considered the narrator's speech.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"narrator_enabled=true\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003e-d \"narrator_enabled=false\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003enarrator_voice_gen\u003c/strong\u003e: The WAV file name for the narrator's voice.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"narrator_voice_gen=male_01.wav\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003etext_not_inside\u003c/strong\u003e: Specify the handling of lines not inside double quotes or asterisks, for the narrator feature. Options:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003echaracter\u003c/strong\u003e: Treat as character speech.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003enarrator\u003c/strong\u003e: Treat as narrator speech.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"text_not_inside=character\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003e-d \"text_not_inside=narrator\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003elanguage\u003c/strong\u003e: Choose the language for TTS. Options:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ear\u003c/code\u003e Arabic\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003ezh-cn\u003c/code\u003e Chinese (Simplified)\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003ecs\u003c/code\u003e Czech\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003enl\u003c/code\u003e Dutch\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003een\u003c/code\u003e English\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003efr\u003c/code\u003e French\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003ede\u003c/code\u003e German\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003ehi\u003c/code\u003e Hindi (Please see this re Hindi support, which is very limited \u003ca class=\"issue-link js-issue-link\" data-error-text=\"Failed to load title\" data-id=\"2251198621\" data-permission-text=\"Title is private\" data-url=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178\" data-hovercard-type=\"issue\" data-hovercard-url=\"/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178/hovercard\" href=\"https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178\"\u003e#178\u003c/a\u003e) \u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003ehu\u003c/code\u003e Hungarian\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eit\u003c/code\u003e Italian\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eja\u003c/code\u003e Japanese\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eko\u003c/code\u003e Korean\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003epl\u003c/code\u003e Polish\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003ept\u003c/code\u003e Portuguese\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003eru\u003c/code\u003e Russian\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003ees\u003c/code\u003e Spanish\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003etr\u003c/code\u003e Turkish\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"language=en\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003eoutput_file_name\u003c/strong\u003e: The name of the output file (excluding the .wav extension).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"output_file_name=myoutputfile\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003eoutput_file_timestamp\u003c/strong\u003e: Add a timestamp to the output file name. If true, each file will have a unique timestamp; otherwise, the same file name will be overwritten each time you generate TTS.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"output_file_timestamp=true\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003e-d \"output_file_timestamp=false\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003eautoplay\u003c/strong\u003e: Enable or disable playing the generated TTS to your standard sound output device at time of TTS generation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"autoplay=true\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ccode\u003e-d \"autoplay=false\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 \u003cstrong\u003eautoplay_volume\u003c/strong\u003e: Set the autoplay volume. Should be between 0.1 and 1.0. Needs to be specified in the JSON request even if autoplay is false.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e-d \"autoplay_volume=0.8\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe API returns a JSON object with the following properties:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003estatus\u003c/strong\u003e Indicates whether the generation was successful (generate-success) or failed (generate-failure).\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eoutput_file_path\u003c/strong\u003e The on-disk location of the generated WAV file.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eoutput_file_url\u003c/strong\u003e The HTTP location for accessing the generated WAV file for browser playback.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eoutput_cache_url\u003c/strong\u003e The HTTP location for accessing the generated WAV file as a pushed download.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eExample JSON TTS Generation Response:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e{\"status\":\"generate-success\",\"output_file_path\":\"C:\\\\text-generation-webui\\\\extensions\\\\alltalk_tts\\\\outputs\\\\myoutputfile_1704141936.wav\",\"output_file_url\":\"http://127.0.0.1:7851/audio/myoutputfile_1704141936.wav\",\"output_cache_url\":\"http://127.0.0.1:7851/audiocache/myoutputfile_1704141936.wav\"}\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e🟠 TTS Generation Endpoint (Streaming Generation)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--tts-generation-endpoint-streaming-generation\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🟠 TTS Generation Endpoint (Streaming Generation)\" href=\"#-tts-generation-endpoint-streaming-generation\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eStreaming TTS generation does NOT support Narration and will generate an audio stream. Streaming TTS speech JavaScript Example:\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eURL: \u003ccode\u003ehttp://localhost:7851/api/tts-generate-streaming\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e - Method: \u003ccode\u003ePOST\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e - Content-Type: \u003ccode\u003eapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded\u003c/code\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"// Example parameters\nconst text = \u0026quot;Here is some text\u0026quot;;\nconst voice = \u0026quot;female_01.wav\u0026quot;;\nconst language = \u0026quot;en\u0026quot;;\nconst outputFile = \u0026quot;stream_output.wav\u0026quot;;\n// Encode the text for URL\nconst encodedText = encodeURIComponent(text);\n// Create the streaming URL\nconst streamingUrl = `http://localhost:7851/api/tts-generate-streaming?text=${encodedText}\u0026amp;voice=${voice}\u0026amp;language=${language}\u0026amp;output_file=${outputFile}`;\n// Create and play the audio element\nconst audioElement = new Audio(streamingUrl);\naudioElement.play(); // Play the audio stream directly\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e// Example parameters\nconst text = \"Here is some text\";\nconst voice = \"female_01.wav\";\nconst language = \"en\";\nconst outputFile = \"stream_output.wav\";\n// Encode the text for URL\nconst encodedText = encodeURIComponent(text);\n// Create the streaming URL\nconst streamingUrl = `http://localhost:7851/api/tts-generate-streaming?text=${encodedText}\u0026amp;voice=${voice}\u0026amp;language=${language}\u0026amp;output_file=${outputFile}`;\n// Create and play the audio element\nconst audioElement = new Audio(streamingUrl);\naudioElement.play(); // Play the audio stream directly\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eText (text):\u003c/strong\u003e This is the actual text you want to convert to speech. It should be a string and must be URL-encoded to ensure that special characters (like spaces and punctuation) are correctly transmitted in the URL. Example: \u003ccode\u003eHello World\u003c/code\u003e becomes \u003ccode\u003eHello%20World\u003c/code\u003e when URL-encoded.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVoice (voice):\u003c/strong\u003e This parameter specifies the voice type to be used for the TTS. The value should match one of the available voice options in AllTalks voices folder. This is a string representing the file, like \u003ccode\u003efemale_01.wav\u003c/code\u003e.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLanguage (language):\u003c/strong\u003e This setting determines the language in which the text should be spoken. A two-letter language code (like \u003ccode\u003een\u003c/code\u003e for English, \u003ccode\u003efr\u003c/code\u003e for French, etc.).\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOutput File (output_file):\u003c/strong\u003e This parameter names the output file where the audio will be streamed. It should be a string representing the file name, such as \u003ccode\u003estream_output.wav\u003c/code\u003e. AllTalk will not save this as a file in its outputs folder.\u003cbr\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 Update

TTS Generator - Various interface bugs & filtering options cleaned up.
TTS Generator - TTSDiff now scans generated text and TTS for errors.
TTS Generator - TTSSRT now creates subtitle files for video production e.g. a Youtube video.
Finetune - Now uses a customised tokenizer to deal with Japanese.
Finetune - Pre flight check and warning messages.
Finetune - Extra documentation and warnings.
Entire file structure has been re-organised to simplify management and future changes.
Documentation (built in and Github) has been rewritten/tidied up.
Requirements files have been cleaned up and simplified.
ATsetup has been re-written as necessary with additional options.
DeepSpeed moved up to version 14.
Standalone Application moved to PyTorch 2.2.1.
Nvidia CUDA Toolkit installation is NO LONGER needed (other than to compile DeepSpeed on Linux)

Tested on Linux and Windows." class="Link--secondary" href="/erew123/alltalk_tts/commit/a075495a0a580b8a08263ff779871777028efcff">Large Update</a></div></div></td><td><div <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">AllTalk version 2</h2><a id="user-content-alltalk-version-2" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: AllTalk version 2" href="#alltalk-version-2"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/tree/alltalkbeta">Click here to go to AllTalk Version 2</a></h1><a id="user-content-click-here-to-go-to-alltalk-version-2" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Click here to go to AllTalk Version 2" href="#click-here-to-go-to-alltalk-version-2"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">AllTalk v2 significantly enhances v1, introducing new features while addressing many previous issues. Although still evolving, v2 offers a stable build and is the recommended version for most users as this is where update and development work is now focused.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><g-emoji class="g-emoji" alias="warning">⚠️</g-emoji> AllTalk v1 - Everything BELOW this point is for AllTalk v1<g-emoji class="g-emoji" alias="warning">⚠️</g-emoji></h2><a id="user-content-️-alltalk-v1---everything-below-this-point-is-for-alltalk-v1️" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚠️ AllTalk v1 - Everything BELOW this point is for AllTalk v1⚠️" href="#️-alltalk-v1---everything-below-this-point-is-for-alltalk-v1️"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">AllTalk version 1 is an updated version of the Coqui_tts extension for Text Generation web UI. Features include:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Can be run as a</strong> <a href="https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/#-quick-setup-text-generation-webui--standalone-installation">standalone application</a> <strong>or part of :</strong>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Text-generation-webui</strong> <a href="https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui">link</a></li>
<li><strong>SillyTavern</strong> <a href="https://github.com/SillyTavern/SillyTavern">link</a></li>
<li><strong>KoboldCPP</strong> <a href="https://github.com/LostRuins/koboldcpp">link</a></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><strong>Simple setup utlilty</strong> Windows & Linux.</li>
<li><strong>API Suite and 3rd Party support via JSON calls:</strong> Can be used with 3rd party applications via JSON calls.</li>
<li><strong>Model Finetuning:</strong> Train the model specifically on a voice of your choosing for better reproduction.</li>
<li><strong>Local/Custom models:</strong> Use any of the XTTSv2 models (API Local and XTTSv2 Local).</li>
<li><strong>Bulk TTS Generator/Editor:</strong> Generate hours of TTS into one big file or have something read back to you <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hunvXn0mLzc" rel="nofollow">demo</a>.</li>
<li><strong>DeepSpeed:</strong> A 2-3x performance boost generating TTS. <a href="https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/deepspeed.jpg">Screenshot</a></li>
<li><strong>Low VRAM mode:</strong> Great for people with small GPU memory or if your VRAM is filled by your LLM.</li>
<li><strong>Custom Start-up Settings:</strong> Adjust your default start-up settings. <a href="https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/settingsanddocs.jpg">Screenshot</a></li>
<li><strong>Narrarator:</strong> Use different voices for main character and narration. <a href="https://vocaroo.com/18nrv7FR6wuA" rel="nofollow">Example Narration</a></li>
<li><strong>Optional wav file maintenance:</strong> Configurable deletion of old output wav files. <a href="https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/settingsanddocs.jpg">Screenshot</a></li>
<li><strong>Documentation:</strong> Fully documented with a built in webpage. <a href="https://github.com/erew123/screenshots/raw/main/settingsanddocs.jpg">Screenshot</a></li>
<li><strong>Clear Console output:</strong> Clear command line output for any warnings or issues.</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🛠️ <strong>About this project & me</strong></h3><a id="user-content-️-about-this-project--me" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🛠️ About this project & me" href="#️-about-this-project--me"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">AllTalk is a labour of love that has been developed, supported and sustained in my personal free time. As a solo enthusiast (not a business or team) my resources are inherently limited. This project has been one of my passions, but I must balance it with other commitments.</p>
<p dir="auto">To manage AllTalk sustainably, I prioritize support requests based on their overall impact and the number of users affected. I encourage you to utilize the comprehensive documentation and engage with the AllTalk community discussion area. These resources often provide immediate answers and foster a supportive user network.</p>
<p dir="auto">Should your inquiry extend beyond the documentation, especially if it concerns a bug or feature request, I assure you I’ll offer my best support as my schedule permits. However, please be prepared for varying response times, reflective of the personal dedication I bring to AllTalk. Your understanding and patience in this regard are greatly appreciated.</p>
<p dir="auto">It's important to note that <strong>I am not</strong> the developer of any TTS models utilized by AllTalk, nor do I claim to be an expert on them, including understanding all their nuances, issues, and quirks. For specific TTS model concerns, I’ve provided links to the original developers in the Help section for direct assistance.</p>
<p dir="auto">Thank you for your continued support and understanding.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">💖 Showing Your Support</h3><a id="user-content--showing-your-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 💖 Showing Your Support" href="#-showing-your-support"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If AllTalk has been helpful to you, consider showing your support through a donation on my <a href="https://ko-fi.com/erew123" rel="nofollow">Ko-fi page</a>. Your support is greatly appreciated and helps ensure the continued development and improvement of AllTalk.</p>
