    </clipboard-copy>
  </div></div>
</li>
<li>Start the fine-tuning process with the command:
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>python finetune.py</code><br><br></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">If you're unfamiliar with Python environments and wish to learn more, consider reviewing <strong>Understanding Python Environments Simplified</strong> in the Help section.</p>
</blockquote>
</li>
</ul>
<ol start="3" dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Pre-Flight Checklist</strong>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Go through the pre-flight checklist to ensure readiness. Address any issues flagged as "Fail".</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Post Fine-tuning Actions</strong>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Upon completing fine-tuning, the final tab will guide you on managing your files and relocating your newly trained model to the appropriate directory.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">These steps guide you through the initial preparations, starting the Python environment based on your setup, and the fine-tuning process itself. Ensure all prerequisites are met to facilitate a smooth fine-tuning experience.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⚫ How many Epochs etc is the right amount?</h4><a id="user-content--how-many-epochs-etc-is-the-right-amount" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚫ How many Epochs etc is the right amount?" href="#-how-many-epochs-etc-is-the-right-amount"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">In finetuning the suggested/recommended amount of epochs, batch size, evaluation percent etc is already set. However, there is no absolutely correct answer to what the settings should be, it all depends on what you are doing.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>If you just want to train a normal human voice that is in an existing language, for most people’s needs, the base settings would work fine. You may choose to increase the epochs up to maybe 20, or run a second round of training if needed.</li>
<li>If you were training an entirely new language, you would need a huge amount of training data and it requires around 1000 epochs (based on things I can find around the internet of people who tried this).</li>
<li>If you are training a cartoon style voice in an existing language, it may need well upwards of 40 epochs until it can reproduce that voice with some success.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">There are no absolute correct settings, as there are too many variables, ranging from the amount of samples you are using (5 minutes worth? 4 hours worth? etc), if they are similar samples to what the AI model already understands, so on and so forth. Coqui whom originally trained the model usually say something along the lines of, once you’ve trained it X amount, if it sounds good then you are done and if it doesn’t, train it more.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⚫ Evaluation Data Percentage</h4><a id="user-content--evaluation-data-percentage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚫ Evaluation Data Percentage" href="#-evaluation-data-percentage"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">In the process of finetuning, it's crucial to balance the data used for training the model against the data reserved for evaluating its performance. Typically, a portion of the dataset is set aside as an 'evaluation set' to assess the model's capabilities in dealing with unseen data. On Step 1 of finetuning you have the option to adjust this evaluation data percentage, offering more control over your model training process.<br><br>
<strong>Why Adjust the Evaluation Percentage?</strong><br><br>
Adjusting the evaluation percentage <strong>can</strong> be beneficial in scenarios with limited voice samples. When dealing with a smaller dataset, allocating a slightly larger portion to training could enhance the model's ability to learn from these scarce samples. Conversely, with abundant data, a higher evaluation percentage might be more appropriate to rigorously test the model's performance. There are currently no absolutely optimal split percentages as it varies by dataset.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Default Setting:</strong> The default evaluation percentage is set at 15%, which is a balanced choice for most datasets.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Adjustable Range:</strong> Users can now adjust this percentage, but it’s generally recommend keeping it between 5% and 30%.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Lower Bound:</strong> A minimum of 5% ensures that there's enough data to evaluate model performance.</li>
<li><strong>Upper Bound:</strong> Its suggested not exceeding 30% for evaluation to avoid limiting the amount of data available for training.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Understanding the Impact:</strong> Before adjusting this setting, it's important to understand its impact on model training and evaluation. Incorrect adjustments can lead to suboptimal model performance.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Gradual Adjustments:</strong> For those unfamiliar with the process, we recommend reading up on training data and training sets, then making small, incremental changes and observing their effects.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Data Quality:</strong> Regardless of the split, the quality of the audio data is paramount. Ensure that your datasets are built from good quality audio with enough data within them.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⚫ Using a Finetuned model in Text-generation-webui</h4><a id="user-content--using-a-finetuned-model-in-text-generation-webui" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚫ Using a Finetuned model in Text-generation-webui" href="#-using-a-finetuned-model-in-text-generation-webui"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">At the end of the finetune process, you will have an option to <code>Compact and move model to /trainedmodel/</code> this will compact the raw training file and move it to <code>/model/trainedmodel/</code>. When AllTalk starts up within Text-generation-webui, if it finds a model in this location a new loader will appear in the interface for <code>XTTSv2 FT</code> and you can use this to load your finetuned model. <br><br><strong>Be careful</strong> not to train a new model from the base model, then overwrite your current <code>/model/trainedmodel/</code> <strong>if</strong> you want a seperately trained model. This is why there is an <code>OPTION B</code> to move your just trained model to <code>/models/lastfinetuned/</code>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⚫ Training one model with multiple voices</h4><a id="user-content--training-one-model-with-multiple-voices" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚫ Training one model with multiple voices" href="#-training-one-model-with-multiple-voices"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">At the end of the finetune process, you will have an option to <code>Compact and move model to /trainedmodel/</code> this will compact the raw training file and move it to <code>/model/trainedmodel/</code>. This model will become available when you start up finetuning. You will have a choice to train the Base Model or the <code>Existing finetuned model</code> (which is the one in <code>/model/trainedmodel/</code>). So you can use this to keep further training this model with additional voices, then copying it back to <code>/model/trainedmodel/</code> at the end of training.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⚫ Do I need to keep the raw training data/model?</h4><a id="user-content--do-i-need-to-keep-the-raw-training-datamodel" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚫ Do I need to keep the raw training data/model?" href="#-do-i-need-to-keep-the-raw-training-datamodel"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If you've compacted and moved your model, its highly unlikely you would want to keep that data, however the choice is there to keep it if you wish. It will be between 5-10GB in size, so most people will want to delete it.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⚫ I have deeper questions about training the XTTS model, where can I find more information?</h4><a id="user-content--i-have-deeper-questions-about-training-the-xtts-model-where-can-i-find-more-information" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚫ I have deeper questions about training the XTTS model, where can I find more information?" href="#-i-have-deeper-questions-about-training-the-xtts-model-where-can-i-find-more-information"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If you have deeper questions about the XTTS model, its capabilites, the training process etc, anything thats not covered within the above text or the interface of <code>finetune.py</code>, please use the following links to research Coqui's documentation on the XTTS model.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://docs.coqui.ai/en/latest/models/xtts.html" rel="nofollow">https://docs.coqui.ai/en/latest/models/xtts.html</a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS">https://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS</a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS/discussions">https://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS/discussions</a></li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ AllTalk TTS Generator</h3><a id="user-content--alltalk-tts-generator" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ AllTalk TTS Generator" href="#-alltalk-tts-generator"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">AllTalk TTS Generator is the solution for converting large volumes of text into speech using the voice of your choice. Whether you're creating audio content or just want to hear text read aloud, the TTS Generator is equipped to handle it all efficiently. Please see here for a quick <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hunvXn0mLzc" rel="nofollow">demo</a><br><br>The link to open the TTS generator can be found on the built-in Settings and Documentation page.<br><br><strong>DeepSpeed</strong> is <strong>highly</strong> recommended to speed up generation. <strong>Low VRAM</strong> would be best turned off and your LLM model unloaded from your GPU VRAM (unload your model). <strong>No Playback</strong> will reduce memory overhead on very large generations (15,000 words or more). Splitting <strong>Export to Wav</strong> into smaller groups will also reduce memory overhead at the point of exporting your wav files (so good for low memory systems).</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Estimated Throughput</h4><a id="user-content--estimated-throughput" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Estimated Throughput" href="#-estimated-throughput"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This will vary by system for a multitude of reasons, however, while generating a 58,000 word document to TTS, with DeepSpeed enabled, LowVram disabled, splitting size 2 and on an Nvidia RTX 4070, throughput was around 1,000 words per minute. Meaning, this took 1 hour to generate the TTS. Exporting to combined wavs took about 2-3 minutes total.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Quick Start</h4><a id="user-content--quick-start" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Quick Start" href="#-quick-start"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Text Input:</strong> Enter the text you wish to convert into speech in the 'Text Input' box.</li>
<li><strong>Generate TTS:</strong> Hit this to start the text-to-speech conversion.</li>
<li><strong>Pause/Resume:</strong> Used to pause and resume the playback of the initial generation of wavs or the stream.</li>
<li><strong>Stop Playback:</strong> This will stop the current audio playing back. It does not stop the text from being generated however.
Once you have sent text off to be generated, either as a stream or wav file generation, the TTS server will remain busy until this process has competed. As such, think carefully as to how much you want to send to the server.
If you are generating wav files and populating the queue, you can generate one lot of text to speech, then input your next lot of text and it will continue adding to the list.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Customization and Preferences</h4><a id="user-content--customization-and-preferences" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Customization and Preferences" href="#-customization-and-preferences"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Character Voice:</strong> Choose the voice that will read your text.</li>
<li><strong>Language:</strong> Select the language of your text.</li>
<li><strong>Chunk Sizes:</strong> Decide the size of text chunks for generation. Smaller sizes are recommended for better TTS quality.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Interface and Accessibility</h4><a id="user-content--interface-and-accessibility" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Interface and Accessibility" href="#-interface-and-accessibility"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Dark/Light Mode:</strong> Switch between themes for your visual comfort.</li>
<li><strong>Word Count and Generation Queue:</strong> Keep track of the word count and the generation progress.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ TTS Generation Modes</h4><a id="user-content--tts-generation-modes" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ TTS Generation Modes" href="#-tts-generation-modes"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Wav Chunks:</strong> Perfect for creating audio books, or anything you want to keep long term. Breaks down your text into manageable wav files and queues them up. Generation begins automatically, and playback will start after a few chunks have been prepared ahead. You can set the volume to 0 if you don’t want to hear playback. With Wav chunks, you can edit and/or regenerate portions of the TTS as needed.</li>
<li><strong>Streaming:</strong> For immediate playback without the ability to save. Ideal for on-the-fly speech generation and listening. This will not generate wav files and it will play back through your browser. You cannot stop the server generating the TTS once it has been sent.<br><br>
With wav chunks you can either playback “In Browser” which is the web page you are on, or “On Server” which is through the console/terminal where AllTalk is running from, or "No Playback". Only generation “In Browser” can play back smoothly and populate the Generated TTS List. Setting the Volume will affect the volume level played back both “In Browser” and “On Server”.<br><br>
For generating <strong>large amounts of TTS</strong>, it's recommended to select the <strong>No Playback</strong> option. This setting minimizes the memory usage in your web browser by avoiding the loading and playing of audio files directly within the browser, which is particularly beneficial for handling extensive audio generations. The definition of large will vary depending on your system RAM availability (will update when I have more information as to guidelines). Once the audio is generated, you can export your list to JSON (for safety) and use the <strong>Play List</strong> option to play back your audio.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Playback and List Management</h4><a id="user-content--playback-and-list-management" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Playback and List Management" href="#-playback-and-list-management"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Playback Controls:</strong> Utilize 'Play List' to start from the beginning or 'Stop Playback' to halt at any time.</li>
<li><strong>Custom Start:</strong> Jump into your list at a specific ID to hear a particular section.</li>
<li><strong>Regeneration and Editing:</strong> If a chunk isn't quite right, you can opt to regenerate it or edit the text directly. Click off the text to save changes and hit regenerate for the specific line.</li>
<li><strong>Export/Import List:</strong> Save your TTS list as a JSON file or import one. Note: Existing wav files are needed for playback. Exporting is handy if you want to take your files away into another program and have a list of which wav is which, or if you keep your audio files, but want to come back at a later date, edit one or two lines, regenerate the speech and re-combine the wav’s into one new long wav.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Exporting Your Audio</h4><a id="user-content--exporting-your-audio" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Exporting Your Audio" href="#-exporting-your-audio"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Export to WAV:</strong> Combine all generated TTS from the list, into one single WAV file for easy download and distribution. Its always recommended to export your list to a JSON before exporting, so that you have a backup, should something go wrong. You can simply re-import the list and try exporting again.<br><br>When exporting, there is a file size limit of 1GB and as such you have the option to choose how many files to include in each block of audio exported. 600 is just on the limit of 1GB, depending on the average file size, so 500 or less is a good amount to work with. You can combine the generated files after if you wish, in Audacity or similar.<br><br>Additionally, lower export batches will lower the memory requirements, so if your system is low on memory (maybe 8 or 16GB system), you can use smaller export batches to keep the memory requirement down.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Exporting Subtitles (SRT file)</h4><a id="user-content--exporting-subtitles-srt-file" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Exporting Subtitles (SRT file)" href="#-exporting-subtitles-srt-file"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Export SRT:</strong> This will scan through all wav files in your list and generate a subtitles file that will match your exported wav file.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Analyzing generated TTS for errors</h4><a id="user-content--analyzing-generated-tts-for-errors" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Analyzing generated TTS for errors" href="#-analyzing-generated-tts-for-errors"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Analyze TTS:</strong> This will scan through all wav files comparing each ID's orignal text with the TTS generated for that ID and then flag up inconsistences. Its important to understand this is a <strong>best effort</strong> process and <strong>not 100% perfect</strong>, for example:<br><br>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Your text may have the word <code>their</code> and the automated routine that listens to your generated TTS interprets the word as <code>there</code>, aka a spelling difference.</li>
<li>Your text may have <code>Examples are:</code> (note the colon) and the automated routine that listens to your generated TTS interprets the word as "Examples are` (note no colon as you cannot sound out a colon in TTS), aka a punctuation difference.</li>
<li>Your text may have <code>There are 100 items</code> and the automated routine that listens to your generated TTS interprets the word as <code>There are one hundred items</code>, aka numbers vs the number written out in words.</li>
<li>There will be other examples such as double quotes. As I say, please remember this is a <strong>best effort</strong> to help you identify issues.<br></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">As such, there is a <code>% Accuracy</code> setting. This uses a couple of methods to try find things that are similar e.g. taking the <code>their</code> and <code>there</code> example from above, it would identify that they both sound the same, so even if the text says <code>their</code> and the AI listening to the generated TTS interprets the word as <code>there</code>, it will realise that both sound the same/are similar so there is no need to flag that as an error. However, there are limits to this and some things may slip through or get picked up when you would prefer them not to be flagged.</p>
<p dir="auto">The higher the accuracy you choose, the more things it will flag up, however you may get more unwanted detections. The lower the less detections. Based on my few tests, accuracy settings between 96 to 98 seem to generally give the best results. However, I would highly recommend you test out a small 10-20 line text and test out the <strong>Analyze TTS</strong> button to get a feel for how it responds to different settings, as well as things it flags up.</p>
<p dir="auto">You will be able to see the ID's and Text (orignal and as interpreted) by looking at the terminal/command prompt window.</p>
<p dir="auto">The Analyze TTS feature uses the Whisper Larger-v2 AI engine, which will download on first use if necessary. This will require about 2.5GB's of disk space and could take a few minutes to download, depending on your internet connection.</p>
<p dir="auto">You can use this feature on systems that do not have an Nvidia GPU, however, unless you have a very powerful CPU, expect it to be slow.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Tricks to get the model to say things correctly</h4><a id="user-content--tricks-to-get-the-model-to-say-things-correctly" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Tricks to get the model to say things correctly" href="#-tricks-to-get-the-model-to-say-things-correctly"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Sometimes the AI model won’t say something the way that you want it to. It could be because it’s a new word, an acronym or just something it’s not good at for whatever reason. There are some tricks you can use to improve the chances of it saying something correctly.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Adding pauses</strong><br>
You can use semi-colons ";" and colons ":" to create a pause, similar to a period "." which can be helpful with some splitting issues.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Acronyms</strong><br>
Not all acronyms are going to be pronounced correctly. Let’s work with the word <code>ChatGPT</code>. We know it is pronounced <code>"Chat G P T"</code> but when presented to the model, it doesn’t know how to break it down correctly. So, there are a few ways we could get it to break out "Chat" and the G P and T. e.g.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>Chat G P T.</code>
<code>Chat G,P,T.</code>
<code>Chat G.P.T.</code>
<code>Chat G-P-T.</code>
<code>Chat gee pee tea</code></p>
<p dir="auto">All bar the last one are using ways within the English language to split out "Chat" into one word being pronounced and then split the G, P and T into individual letters. The final example, which is to use Phonetics will sound perfectly fine, but clearly would look wrong as far as human readable text goes. The phonetics method is very useful in edge cases where pronunciation difficult.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⬜ Notes on Usage</h4><a id="user-content--notes-on-usage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⬜ Notes on Usage" href="#-notes-on-usage"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>For seamless TTS generation, it's advised to keep text chunks under 250 characters, which you can control with the Chunk sizes.</li>
<li>Generated audio can be played back from the list, which also highlights the currently playing chunk.</li>
<li>The TTS Generator remembers your settings, so you can pick up where you left off even after refreshing the page.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 API Suite and JSON-CURL</h3><a id="user-content--api-suite-and-json-curl" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 API Suite and JSON-CURL" href="#-api-suite-and-json-curl"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠Overview</h3><a id="user-content-overview" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠Overview" href="#overview"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The Text-to-Speech (TTS) Generation API allows you to generate speech from text input using various configuration options. This API supports both character and narrator voices, providing flexibility for creating dynamic and engaging audio content.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Ready Endpoint<br></h4><a id="user-content--ready-endpoint" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Ready Endpoint" href="#-ready-endpoint"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Check if the Text-to-Speech (TTS) service is ready to accept requests.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/ready</code><br> - Method: <code>GET</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>curl -X GET "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/ready"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Response: <code>Ready</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Voices List Endpoint<br></h4><a id="user-content--voices-list-endpoint" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Voices List Endpoint" href="#-voices-list-endpoint"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Retrieve a list of available voices for generating speech.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/voices</code><br> - Method: <code>GET</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>curl -X GET "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/voices"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">JSON return: <code>{"voices": ["voice1.wav", "voice2.wav", "voice3.wav"]}</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Current Settings Endpoint<br></h4><a id="user-content--current-settings-endpoint" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Current Settings Endpoint" href="#-current-settings-endpoint"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Retrieve a list of available voices for generating speech.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/currentsettings</code><br> - Method: <code>GET</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>curl -X GET "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/currentsettings"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">JSON return: <code>{"models_available":[{"name":"Coqui","model_name":"API TTS"},{"name":"Coqui","model_name":"API Local"},{"name":"Coqui","model_name":"XTTSv2 Local"}],"current_model_loaded":"XTTSv2 Local","deepspeed_available":true,"deepspeed_status":true,"low_vram_status":true,"finetuned_model":false}</code></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>name & model_name</code> = listing the currently available models.<br>
<code>current_model_loaded</code> = what model is currently loaded into VRAM.<br>
<code>deepspeed_available</code> = was DeepSpeed detected on startup and available to be activated.<br>
<code>deepspeed_status</code> = If DeepSpeed was detected, is it currently activated.<br>
<code>low_vram_status</code> = Is Low VRAM currently enabled.<br>
<code>finetuned_model</code> = Was a finetuned model detected. (XTTSv2 FT).<br></p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Preview Voice Endpoint</h4><a id="user-content--preview-voice-endpoint" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Preview Voice Endpoint" href="#-preview-voice-endpoint"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Generate a preview of a specified voice with hardcoded settings.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/previewvoice/</code><br> - Method: <code>POST</code><br> - Content-Type: <code>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/previewvoice/" -F "voice=female_01.wav"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Replace <code>female_01.wav</code> with the name of the voice sample you want to hear.</p>
<p dir="auto">JSON return: <code>{"status": "generate-success", "output_file_path": "/path/to/outputs/api_preview_voice.wav", "output_file_url": "http://127.0.0.1:7851/audio/api_preview_voice.wav"}</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Switching Model Endpoint<br></h4><a id="user-content--switching-model-endpoint" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Switching Model Endpoint" href="#-switching-model-endpoint"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/reload</code><br> - Method: <code>POST</code><br><br>
<code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/reload?tts_method=API%20Local"</code><br>
<code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/reload?tts_method=API%20TTS"</code><br>
<code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/reload?tts_method=XTTSv2%20Local"</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto">Switch between the 3 models respectively.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/reload?tts_method=XTTSv2%20FT"</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto">If you have a finetuned model in <code>/models/trainedmodel/</code> (will error otherwise)</p>
<p dir="auto">JSON return <code>{"status": "model-success"}</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Switch DeepSpeed Endpoint<br></h4><a id="user-content--switch-deepspeed-endpoint" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Switch DeepSpeed Endpoint" href="#-switch-deepspeed-endpoint"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/deepspeed</code><br> - Method: <code>POST</code><br><br>
<code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/deepspeed?new_deepspeed_value=True"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Replace True with False to disable DeepSpeed mode.</p>
<p dir="auto">JSON return <code>{"status": "deepspeed-success"}</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Switching Low VRAM Endpoint<br></h4><a id="user-content--switching-low-vram-endpoint" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Switching Low VRAM Endpoint" href="#-switching-low-vram-endpoint"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/lowvramsetting</code><br> - Method: <code>POST</code><br><br>
<code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/lowvramsetting?new_low_vram_value=True"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Replace True with False to disable Low VRAM mode.</p>
<p dir="auto">JSON return <code>{"status": "lowvram-success"}</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 TTS Generation Endpoint (Standard Generation)</h3><a id="user-content--tts-generation-endpoint-standard-generation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 TTS Generation Endpoint (Standard Generation)" href="#-tts-generation-endpoint-standard-generation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Streaming endpoint details are further down the page.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>URL: <code>http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/tts-generate</code><br> - Method: <code>POST</code><br> - Content-Type: <code>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</code><br></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Example command lines (Standard Generation)</h3><a id="user-content--example-command-lines-standard-generation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Example command lines (Standard Generation)" href="#-example-command-lines-standard-generation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Standard TTS generation supports Narration and will generate a wav file/blob. Standard TTS speech Example (standard text) generating a time-stamped file<br></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/tts-generate" -d "text_input=All of this is text spoken by the character. This is text not inside quotes, though that doesnt matter in the slightest" -d "text_filtering=standard" -d "character_voice_gen=female_01.wav" -d "narrator_enabled=false" -d "narrator_voice_gen=male_01.wav" -d "text_not_inside=character" -d "language=en" -d "output_file_name=myoutputfile" -d "output_file_timestamp=true" -d "autoplay=true" -d "autoplay_volume=0.8"</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto">Narrator Example (standard text) generating a time-stamped file</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:7851/api/tts-generate" -d "text_input=*This is text spoken by the narrator* \"This is text spoken by the character\". This is text not inside quotes." -d "text_filtering=standard" -d "character_voice_gen=female_01.wav" -d "narrator_enabled=true" -d "narrator_voice_gen=male_01.wav" -d "text_not_inside=character" -d "language=en" -d "output_file_name=myoutputfile" -d "output_file_timestamp=true" -d "autoplay=true" -d "autoplay_volume=0.8"</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto">Note that if your text that needs to be generated contains double quotes you will need to escape them with <code>\"</code> (Please see the narrator example).</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 Request Parameters</h3><a id="user-content--request-parameters" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 Request Parameters" href="#-request-parameters"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>text_input</strong>: The text you want the TTS engine to produce. Use escaped double quotes for character speech and asterisks for narrator speech if using the narrator function. Example:</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "text_input=*This is text spoken by the narrator* \"This is text spoken by the character\". This is text not inside quotes."</code></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>text_filtering</strong>: Filter for text. Options:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>none</strong> No filtering. Whatever is sent will go over to the TTS engine as raw text, which may result in some odd sounds with some special characters.<br></li>
<li><strong>standard</strong> Human-readable text and a basic level of filtering, just to clean up some special characters.<br></li>
<li><strong>html</strong> HTML content. Where you are using HTML entity's like "<br></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "text_filtering=none"</code><br>
<code>-d "text_filtering=standard"</code><br>
<code>-d "text_filtering=html"</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto">Example:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Standard Example</strong>: <code>*This is text spoken by the narrator* "This is text spoken by the character" This is text not inside quotes.</code><br></li>
<li><strong>HTML Example</strong>: <code>&ast;This is text spoken by the narrator&ast; &quot;This is text spoken by the character&quot; This is text not inside quotes.</code><br></li>
<li><strong>None</strong>: <code>Will just pass whatever characters/text you send at it.</code><br></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>character_voice_gen</strong>: The WAV file name for the character's voice.<br></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "character_voice_gen=female_01.wav"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>narrator_enabled</strong>: Enable or disable the narrator function. If true, minimum text filtering is set to standard. Anything between double quotes is considered the character's speech, and anything between asterisks is considered the narrator's speech.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "narrator_enabled=true"</code><br>
<code>-d "narrator_enabled=false"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>narrator_voice_gen</strong>: The WAV file name for the narrator's voice.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "narrator_voice_gen=male_01.wav"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>text_not_inside</strong>: Specify the handling of lines not inside double quotes or asterisks, for the narrator feature. Options:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>character</strong>: Treat as character speech.<br></li>
<li><strong>narrator</strong>: Treat as narrator speech.<br></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "text_not_inside=character"</code><br>
<code>-d "text_not_inside=narrator"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>language</strong>: Choose the language for TTS. Options:</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>ar</code> Arabic<br>
<code>zh-cn</code> Chinese (Simplified)<br>
<code>cs</code> Czech<br>
<code>nl</code> Dutch<br>
<code>en</code> English<br>
<code>fr</code> French<br>
<code>de</code> German<br>
<code>hi</code> Hindi (Please see this re Hindi support, which is very limited <a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="2251198621" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178/hovercard" href="https://github.com/erew123/alltalk_tts/issues/178" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#178</a>) <br>
<code>hu</code> Hungarian<br>
<code>it</code> Italian<br>
<code>ja</code> Japanese<br>
<code>ko</code> Korean<br>
<code>pl</code> Polish<br>
<code>pt</code> Portuguese<br>
<code>ru</code> Russian<br>
<code>es</code> Spanish<br>
<code>tr</code> Turkish<br></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "language=en"</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>output_file_name</strong>: The name of the output file (excluding the .wav extension).</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "output_file_name=myoutputfile"</code><br></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>output_file_timestamp</strong>: Add a timestamp to the output file name. If true, each file will have a unique timestamp; otherwise, the same file name will be overwritten each time you generate TTS.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "output_file_timestamp=true"</code><br>
<code>-d "output_file_timestamp=false"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>autoplay</strong>: Enable or disable playing the generated TTS to your standard sound output device at time of TTS generation.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "autoplay=true"</code><br>
<code>-d "autoplay=false"</code></p>
<p dir="auto">🟠 <strong>autoplay_volume</strong>: Set the autoplay volume. Should be between 0.1 and 1.0. Needs to be specified in the JSON request even if autoplay is false.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>-d "autoplay_volume=0.8"</code></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 TTS Generation Response</h3><a id="user-content--tts-generation-response" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 TTS Generation Response" href="#-tts-generation-response"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The API returns a JSON object with the following properties:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>status</strong> Indicates whether the generation was successful (generate-success) or failed (generate-failure).<br></li>
<li><strong>output_file_path</strong> The on-disk location of the generated WAV file.<br></li>
<li><strong>output_file_url</strong> The HTTP location for accessing the generated WAV file for browser playback.<br></li>
<li><strong>output_cache_url</strong> The HTTP location for accessing the generated WAV file as a pushed download.<br></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Example JSON TTS Generation Response:</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>{"status":"generate-success","output_file_path":"C:\\text-generation-webui\\extensions\\alltalk_tts\\outputs\\myoutputfile_1704141936.wav","output_file_url":"http://127.0.0.1:7851/audio/myoutputfile_1704141936.wav","output_cache_url":"http://127.0.0.1:7851/audiocache/myoutputfile_1704141936.wav"}</code></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🟠 TTS Generation Endpoint (Streaming Generation)</h3><a id="user-content--tts-generation-endpoint-streaming-generation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🟠 TTS Generation Endpoint (Streaming Generation)" href="#-tts-generation-endpoint-streaming-generation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Streaming TTS generation does NOT support Narration and will generate an audio stream. Streaming TTS speech JavaScript Example:<br></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>URL: <code>http://localhost:7851/api/tts-generate-streaming</code><br> - Method: <code>POST</code><br> - Content-Type: <code>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</code><br><br></li>
</ul>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>// Example parameters
const text = "Here is some text";
const voice = "female_01.wav";
const language = "en";
const outputFile = "stream_output.wav";
// Encode the text for URL
const encodedText = encodeURIComponent(text);
// Create the streaming URL
const streamingUrl = `http://localhost:7851/api/tts-generate-streaming?text=${encodedText}&voice=${voice}&language=${language}&output_file=${outputFile}`;
// Create and play the audio element
const audioElement = new Audio(streamingUrl);
audioElement.play(); // Play the audio stream directly
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="// Example parameters
const text = "Here is some text";
const voice = "female_01.wav";
const language = "en";
const outputFile = "stream_output.wav";
// Encode the text for URL
const encodedText = encodeURIComponent(text);
// Create the streaming URL
const streamingUrl = `http://localhost:7851/api/tts-generate-streaming?text=${encodedText}&voice=${voice}&language=${language}&output_file=${outputFile}`;
// Create and play the audio element
const audioElement = new Audio(streamingUrl);
audioElement.play(); // Play the audio stream directly" tabindex="0" role="button">

<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Text (text):</strong> This is the actual text you want to convert to speech. It should be a string and must be URL-encoded to ensure that special characters (like spaces and punctuation) are correctly transmitted in the URL. Example: <code>Hello World</code> becomes <code>Hello%20World</code> when URL-encoded.<br></li>
<li><strong>Voice (voice):</strong> This parameter specifies the voice type to be used for the TTS. The value should match one of the available voice options in AllTalks voices folder. This is a string representing the file, like <code>female_01.wav</code>.<br></li>
<li><strong>Language (language):</strong> This setting determines the language in which the text should be spoken. A two-letter language code (like <code>en</code> for English, <code>fr</code> for French, etc.).<br></li>
<li><strong>Output File (output_file):</strong> This parameter names the output file where the audio will be streamed. It should be a string representing the file name, such as <code>stream_output.wav</code>. AllTalk will not save this as a file in its outputs folder.<br></li>
</ul>

  <h2 class="tmp-mb-3 h4">About</h2>

      <p class="f4 tmp-my-3">
        AllTalk is based on the Coqui TTS engine, similar to the Coqui_tts extension for Text generation webUI, however supports a variety of advanced features, such as a settings page, low VRAM support, DeepSpeed, narrator, model finetuning, custom models, wav file maintenance. It can also be used with 3rd Party software via JSON calls. 
      </p>

    <div id="js-global-screen-reader-notice-assertive" class="sr-only mt-n1" aria-live="assertive" aria-atomic="true"></div>
  


</body></html>

metadata.description: AllTalk is based on the Coqui TTS engine, similar to the Coqui_tts extension for Text generation webUI, however supports a variety of advanced features, such as a settings page, low VRAM support, DeepSpeed, narrator, model finetuning, custom models, wav file maintenance. It can also be used with 3rd Party software via JSON calls.  - GitHub - erew123/alltalk_tts: AllTalk is based on the Coqui TTS engine, similar to the Coqui_tts extension for Text generation webUI, however supports a variety of advanced features, such as a settings page, low VRAM support, DeepSpeed, narrator, model finetuning, custom models, wav file maintenance.
